column7: I appreciate that Sage Intacct is like a one-stop shop; at least it can be for most of our needs. I also really like that Sage has a marketplace full of partners who can help reach specific goals if something within Sage itself isn't fully meeting our needs. I love that it has given us so much more insight into our financial data and has helped us grow and learn as bookkeepers and accountants. The ability to manage multiple entities in one platform has streamlined our processes, saving time and effort. We switched from QuickBooks Enterprise, and the change has been mind-blowing in the best of ways. I have recommended Sage Intacct to many friends and colleagues because of my positive experience.
column8: Prepaid expense module where you have a lot of issues because our accounting changes so regularly.

column7: I appreciate most about Sage Intacct is its powerful mix of automation, flexibility, and real-time financial insights. Being a cloud-based platform, it allows easy access to accurate financial data from anywhere, enabling quicker and more informed decision-making. Its automation capabilities—particularly in areas like accounts payable, revenue recognition, and reporting—significantly reduce manual work and help minimize errors.
column8: One downside of Sage Intacct is that it can have a bit of a learning curve, especially for new users who aren’t familiar with cloud-based accounting systems. Some advanced features and configurations may require training or support to fully utilize.
column10: Sage Intacct helps solve manual accounting inefficiencies by offering a structured UI, strong automation, and real-time financial visibility, making everyday workflows faster and more accurate. It integrates well with other business systems, performs reliably in the cloud, and its intelligent automation and reporting features provide meaningful insights for better decision-making. Sage Intacct Delivers Automation, Flexibility, and Real-Time

column7: We are in Sage InTacct on a daily bases using the Accounts Payable, Cash Management, and Reporting features. I love the interactive reporting that gives me real time results if I get my information in there. With the API tools we are easily able to get transactions transferred from our policy processing platform to InTacct. We worked with a 3rd party consultant to get the API tools even more refined to get the transactions in there exactly how we wanted them. With their Help & Support feature it makes finding answers on how to use the product a brease.
column8: Sage InTacct is a little large for all our uses. I adopted this system from my predecessor and I was not really trained in the full functionality of the tool. With our third party adoption I'm also not able to access the community forum to get information from the community, which can be a little frustrating at time.

column7: Being able to keep multiple tabs open for different entities within the same login is a game changer. I also really appreciate how quickly I can move between modules. The custom reports are extremely helpful once you know how to use them. The CSV uploads for checking accounts, 1099 transaction updates, adding bills, adding invoices, adding GLs, adding vendors, and adding customers are amazing and make those updates much easier to manage.
column8: There isn’t a proper report that clearly shows which bills, or which parts of bills, are included in the 1099 filing. It seems to rely only on vendor payments, so we had to do a lot of back-and-forth to figure it out. Also, when bills or invoices are entered at the top level, different views and reports don’t show which entity they were entered under when you run a report or when you’re viewing the bill/invoice page at the top level.
column10: Our contact at Sage said they’re fixing the issue where, at the top level, you can’t see which Entity a bill or invoice was uploaded to. They also helped a lot with our inter-entity invoicing using the AI Customer emails. Any email sent to the AI Customer email automatically posts as a bill in the Entity associated with that Customer. column7: What I like most about Sage Intacct is its flexibility and strong reporting capabilities. The use of dimensions makes it easy to analyze financial data across departments, projects, or locations without creating an overly complex chart of accounts. I also appreciate how easy it is to drill down into transactions to understand where numbers are coming from. Being cloud-based and integrating well with other systems also helps streamline workflows and collaboration.
column8: One thing I dislike about Sage Intacct is that some processes are not as intuitive as they could be, especially for new users. Certain tasks require several steps or navigating through multiple menus, which can take time to learn. Customizing reports and configurations can also require a deeper understanding of the system, so there can be a learning curve for teams that are new to the platform.
column10: Sage Intacct helps centralize our financial data and automate several accounting processes that used to be more manual. It improves visibility into financial performance by allowing us to track results across different dimensions like departments, projects, or locations. This makes reporting more flexible and helps management get clearer insights when making decisions. It has also made our month-end close and reconciliations more organized and easier to manage. Flexible, Powerful Reporting That Streamlines

column7: There are many factors that contribute to why I like Sage Intacct, but above all, the biggest benefit has been my company’s increased efficiency thanks to Sage’s automation. Sage automates key accounting processes such as accounts payable, invoicing, and reconciliations, which has reduced manual work and helped minimize errors.
column8: While Sage Intacct is a powerful tool, the interface can feel somewhat outdated and unintuitive compared with newer cloud platforms. Training the team and learning all the modules we needed also took more time than we expected, which made the overall ramp-up less efficient.
column10: As our company continues to grow, Sage Intacct has been able to handle the added complexity that comes with it, including our expanding list of vendors, currencies, and locations. column7: Sage Intactt is highly customizable while still being easy to use. When we implemented it, we were able to reduce our chart of accounts by more than 3,000 accounts by using dimensions. Reporting is a breeze, and every staff member on our team has a custom dashboard so they always know exactly where their budget stands and can drill down to the underlying transactions.
column8: I honestly don’t have anything I dislike about Sage Intacct. Overall, it’s been a clear improvement in every way compared with the platform we were using before.
column10: Sage Intacct has taken our reporting to a new level. It saves me a ton of time thanks to automations and third-party applications that sync seamlessly to the general ledger. My non-accounting team members can easily access the information they need, and because it was built for accountants by accountants, I feel confident I can trust the process. column7: I really look forward to all the automation and AI features coming out with Sage Intacct. I love how, in my last job using Sage, reports updated quickly and how it was easy to pull any information needed. I could dive deeper into a number by just clicking it, and the invoice or bill would pop up, which was super convenient. I've really enjoyed using Sage Intacct so far. I've also had a good experience previously using Sage Intacct with bill.com, and we're exploring other integration options with API automation tools and connecting it to our EMR. This implementation has gone great, and I'm excited about getting my hands on the data and dimensions features.

I also like the Global consolidations and easy to customize reports. If there is not a report already available, it can be created.

column7: I like that Sage Intacct is relatively intuitive and easy to navigate. Entries are quite simple, and the software has a lot of additional features we can utilize. I'm excited about the AI copilot functionality and utilizing partners like LEXO for reporting. I also really like that it's quite quick to get to book entries and additional reports, and functions quickly for close. Since switching from CCH, which wasn't very intuitive and had poor reporting, Sage Intacct has been significantly better. The initial setup was good, and working with the implementation team was a great partnership. Overall, it's been a really good tool for us.
column8: I would like more interactive functionality within Sage Intacct's core system, instead of needing it as an add-on for recording.
column10: I use Sage Intacct for RTL accounting, budget review, and reporting. It's intuitive and easy to navigate, with simple entries, and offers many additional features. Highly Intuitive with Room for

column7: I love how Sage Intacct has transformed our accounting functions, especially with the ability to edit general ledger journal entries, which has been fantastic. It used to be a hassle with reversals when there were errors, but now not having to make so many duplicate entries to fix one issue has been a game changer. My favorite feature is definitely the bank recons; the connection to the cloud and the rule setup have saved me days on my bank recons because I have multiple accounts. The initial setup was in-depth, but the team that helped me was fantastic, and it was worth it since Sage Intacct has been the best software program I've used. I recently switched from Sage 100, which felt like going from the stone age to the electric age, and that was a fantastic move for us. Plus, the integration with ServiceTitan for PO entry and ARs is pretty smooth, so I really don't need to do much AR in Intacct. All of this makes Sage Intacct a solid 10 out of 10 for me.
column8: I'm trying to think of what we could improve on. We have an integration with a third-party company, and I'd say the most difficult part is the order entry. It is not as easily manageable and editable when you're doing the auditing process as when you're in bills.
column10: I use Sage Intacct for accounting. It solves general ledger issues, reducing duplicate entries. Editing journal entries is easy. Bank recons connect to the cloud, automatically linking and saving me days of work. Transformed Our Accounting

column7: I like that Sage Intacct is in the cloud, making it fast and efficient for processing data compared to our previous software, which was more clunky and took longer. I really like the user interface and how easy it is to navigate around the software to find what you need. I also really like the reporting options Sage Intacct offers. The most valuable user interface option for me is the workflow area, which shows the different steps of how transactions go through the software. It's a great way for me to train new hires on how the system works and how things move throughout Sage Intacct.
column8: I think that on the approved payments screen, we should be able to allow certain types of payments to go through without being approved. For example, record transfers are automatically pulled out of our account, so they're preapproved. However, I'm not able to let those go through preapproved, and I have to approve them.
column10: I use Sage Intacct because it's cloud-based, fast, and efficient for data processing. The workflow feature is great for training new hires, and the user interface is easy to navigate. It replaced our clunky system, enhancing efficiency significantly. Streamlined Accounting with

column7: I love that Sage Intacct has clear application and integrates with most systems I use outside my financial platform. It has resolved the reporting issues we had previously for multi-entity business transactions, shortening the close time tremendously and allowing us a real-time view into our financials on a daily basis.
column8: I use imports in Sage Intacct and initially they would send you an error message when your import had errors, but never described what the error was. My only input on imports would be that they would have a preformatted import that allows unnecessary information to be removed and only necessary information added. Initially, we struggled because we didn’t understand the dimensions and how it would work for us because we were going from one entity to 28 and everything was new to us.
column10: Sage Intacct resolves reporting issues for multi-entity transactions, shortens close time, and provides real-time financial views. I love its integration with other systems. It now defines import errors, but I wish it had a preformatted import option. I highly recommend it for multi-entity or nonprofit needs. Essential for Multi-Entity

column7: I love the scalability of Sage Intacct, especially its automation capabilities through integration and the open API. It's super helpful for partnering with in-house servicing software. I also really like its investigative power, particularly the audit trails, ad hoc reports, and customizable reports that we can create using the interactive customer report writer. The standard out-of-the-box reports are also a big plus, providing great visibility and reporting in our complex environment. These features have been super helpful for maintaining control and satisfying our auditors in a highly regulated space, and they continue to support us as we scale our business.
column8: What could be improved is the advanced consolidation module, specifically with how new it is. We're still working through some quirks as an early adapter. The intercompany relationships are confusing and not straightforward. It would be helpful to have more feedback on setting up intercompany by industry when the environment is transformed or implemented.
column10: Sage Intacct provides visibility and reporting in a complex environment, scales automations through integration, offers audit trails for regulatory control, and delivers ad hoc and customizable reports to investigate solutions, aiding our business's scaling. Scalable Financial Management with

column7: It seems to me that Sage Intacct has many tools and functionalities. We switched to Sage Intacct because it has too many functionalities. While some things may be lacking, it makes up for it with many other functionalities and add-ons that the application has. It helps us a lot in the accounting area when it comes to recording, modifying, and adjusting invoices, as well as in the automated calculation of taxes and the download of complete reports. Additionally, the connection with other applications, like Rippling, allows us to synchronize transactions with corporate credit cards. I also value the greater customization option that Sage Intacct offers to create specific and detailed reports, something we didn't have with our previous software, QuickBooks.
column8: It seems to me that there should be more connectivity with other third parties, for example, with banks, in order to have updated information every day to reconcile accounts more easily.
column10: Sage Intacct automates accounting tasks, reduces manual entry, facilitates report generation, and speeds up invoice creation for clients. Automatización Potente,

Because it is cloud based, and easy to access from anywhere, and it allows teams to spend more time on analysis instead of getting stuck in manual accounting tasks.
column8: One drawback of Sage Intacct is the steep learning curve for new users. It can also get expensive, especially when you need add on modules, and some features still require help from technical support. On top of that, reporting customization and the overall interface can feel complex and, at times, less userfriendly.
column10: Sage Intacct helps address manual accounting inefficiencies, fragmented systems, and a lack of real time visibility. For me, it’s valuable because it automates key processes, improves accuracy, speeds up the month end close, and delivers actionable insights. column7: I like how easy it is to maneuver through the application. The product is intuitive and simple for managers and leaders to drill into the application to find their own answers. The application has allowed us to be almost entirely paperless and simplifies closing the books on a monthly basis. In addition by granting our auditors read only access to the application our audit has become more efficient and less burdensome for staff as auditors can drill and pull supporting documents themselves cutting down on requests for additional supporting documents.
column8: Custom report writing is a bit cumbersome and takes some learning especially when it comes to understanding reporting groups. When adding new accounts the user needs to be aware that the new account may not be properly mapped to one of the groups and could therefore put reports out of balance.
column10: Allows front line staff to enter purchasing requests that are approved by management cutting down on mistakes and automating the workflow for purchasing. Sage also allows for importing of transactions and transaction activity from the CMS and POS systems which allows for daily and monthly revenue balancing with our core systems thereby cutting down on data entry labor and data entry errors. The internal communication system allows for audit trails and notes to be attached to transactions creating a single source for information related to the financial statements. The collaboration feature keeps things contained in the system and the cloud based solution allows executive management to stay connected when off property. Intuitive, Paperless Accounting That Streamlines Monthly

column10: Sage Intacct is massive and has a lot of features. Because of that, we hired some consultants to help us with the implementation. It took about six months from start to finish. And this included the COVID-19 pandemic during the last month or two of the implementation. Sage Intacct can be customized to your specific needs. Make sure that the consultants and you go over EXACTLY what your specific needs are. And it would be great if the consultants can anticipate what your specific needs are based on the back-and-forth questioning. You may find it difficult to articulate the exact thing that you want from a new accounting software. Another useful thing is to watch the training session videos and take a test drive in the sandbox.

column7:
We moved from QuickBooks to Sage Intacct and the biggest improvement is how clean and structured everything feels. The layout is intuitive, and it’s easier to keep records consistent across AP, AR, cash, and GL, especially when you’re working across multiple entities/properties. I like that transactions are easier to trace (audit trail is clearer), and reporting doesn’t feel like an afterthought.

The modules we use most including AP, AR, cash management, and reporting are straightforward and generally match how an accounting team actually works day-to-day. Import tools and bank-related workflows also save time compared to what we were doing before. And being able to build customized reports (or tweak existing ones) is a big plus because real estate reporting is never “one-size-fits-all.”
column8:
Some functions feel overly permission-dependent, which can slow you down. For example, with cash transfers, we’ve run into situations where you can’t edit a transfer once it’s created unless you have top-level authority, so the only practical option is to reverse and redo it. That works, but it adds extra steps and creates noise that we then need to explain internally.

Also, certain more advanced use cases aren’t as intuitive without training. Consolidations are the main one, Intacct is clearly capable, but it’s not something you can just “figure out” as a team without consistent setup and some structured guidance. Same for project budget management, it can work, but it’s not immediately obvious how to set it up in a way that’s simple for everyone to maintain. If you don’t invest in training and standard processes, different people end up using workarounds, and you lose some of the benefit of having a system like this.
column10:
As a mid-sized real estate firm, our biggest challenge is keeping accounting organized across multiple properties/entities while still closing efficiently. Sage Intacct helps us standardize how transactions are entered, coded, and reviewed, which reduces clean-up work at month-end. It’s easier to track what happened, who did it, and why, so reconciliations and review are much smoother compared to QuickBooks.

It’s also helped with visibility. We can pull consistent reports without re-building spreadsheets from scratch every time, and customized reporting makes it easier to get to property-level detail when we need it (and roll it up when we don’t). AP/AR workflows are clearer, cash activity is easier to follow, and having everything in one place improves handoffs within the team. Overall, it’s made our financial records more reliable and easier to explain, both internally and when working with external parties like lenders or auditors.

column7: The multi entity feature. It is nice only having to do one single journal entry instead of multiple to balance transactions across our 18 entities. It also helps streamline a lot of the other accounts payable and receivable processes.
column8:
The implementation process. They were partnering with NPact at the time of our implementation. We had 4 different representatives throughout the process. I do believe they are using a different company now... Npact did not listen to what we needed. When we met with the new rep and had to explain our business processes again, we were told what our previous rep advised us on wasn't possible and had no effort made to rectify the situation. They did not show us fully how to use the features needed that were included in our package to be trained on and also marked off that they imported our customer records on the implementation checklist but did not do it until we requested multiple times. We were also handed off to Sage support without signing off that implementation was complete and only found out after emailing our representative about issues and being told they could not help us and we needed to contact support. 

We also have not had a good track record with Intacct customer support. They push you to look in the community or help articles that do not give entirely accurate steps to resolve the issue.

column7: I love the ability to title my invoices with specific details, which I find is unavailable in other systems. The reporting in Intacct is easy and searching for information is easy. There are plenty of filters to use and creating your own views make it easy for each individual user. Setting up recurring invoices is quick and easy. Prorating invoice amounts is a cinch with the proration button. Reporting issues is easy through the "Help & Support" tab. The customer support is quick to answer any questions or concerns. You can run payments through the virtual terminal if the invoices are coming from another source.
column8: Updating a customer's address is annoying. If you've already created an invoice or sales order and need to update the address or contact details, you need to recreate the invoice or sales order for it to grab the new details. It doesn't automatically update. Setting up a recurring invoice requires you to add the customer as a recurring customer. If the auto payments aren't run very early in the morning and you have an open invoice for that customer, it will pay all invoices if that customer was set up as recurring. Sometimes you don't want ALL invoices to be paid automatically.

column7: I like Sage Intacct for its strong automation for core accounting processes. The excellent dimensional reporting and financial visibility are really valuable to me. Sage Intacct is scalable for growing US-based businesses, and I find the secure cloud-based access with strong audit controls reassuring. The journal ledger automation streamlines processes and reduces manual errors. It automates vendor processing, approval workflows, and customer invoicing, which significantly cuts down on manual work and saves time. The dimension-based reporting allows for detailed, customizable financial reports, eliminating the need for creating multiple GL accounts. Additionally, automating bank reconciliation is a massive time-saver as bank transactions are automatically matched with system entries, minimizing manual reconciliation efforts.

column10:
Sage Intacct is solving a lot of problems for me in accounting. I don’t have to spend time making all the financial reports manually anymore, Intacct takes care of that, so I can focus more on reviewing the numbers and replying to clients or audit questions. It also lets me pull the supporting docs attached to journal entries, which makes things easier during review or audit.

For AP automation, once bills are posted in Bill.com, Intacct picks them up automatically. It doesn’t miss any details like vendor name or other important info, which is super helpful. Eariler there were issues with Service provider details before using Intacct and now because of Bill integration to intacct there are no issues.

In revenue management, one of my clients was using Maxio for O2C before, but they weren’t happy with it. We moved them to Intacct’s revenue module, and it worked way better. Now, journal entries are posted automatically from the contracts entered in the system. This removed a lot of manual work, and I saved a lot of time on reviews since there’s no manual errors happening. Sage Intacct - Streamlined

column7:
What I like most about Sage Intacct is how user-friendly and efficient it is for managing complex accounting tasks. The interface is clean and intuitive, which makes it easy to navigate even for someone who’s not super tech-savvy. I especially appreciate the automation features—things like account reconciliations and payment processing can be done with minimal manual effort, saving a ton of time.
Another big plus is the real-time reporting. Being able to generate accurate financial reports instantly helps a lot with decision-making and gives better visibility into the company’s financial health. And since it’s cloud-based, I can access it from anywhere, which is super convenient when working remotely or needing to check something on the go.
It also integrates well with other platforms like Salesforce, which streamlines workflows between departments and reduces data entry errors. Overall, it’s a powerful tool that makes accounting more efficient and less stressful
column8:
One thing I dislike about Sage Intacct is that the learning curve can be a bit steep at first, especially for new users who aren’t familiar with accounting systems. Some of the modules feel a little too segmented, and navigating between them isn’t always as smooth as I’d like. It sometimes takes extra clicks to get to the information you need.
Also, while the reporting features are powerful, customizing reports can be tricky without some training or support. I’ve found that certain filters or formatting options aren’t as intuitive as they could be. And occasionally, the system can be a bit slow when processing large volumes of data or running complex reports.
Lastly, the cost of additional modules or integrations can add up quickly, which might be a concern for smaller teams or companies with tight budgets.
column10:
Sage Intacct is helping solve several key challenges in accounting, especially around manual processes and data visibility. Before using it, a lot of time was spent on repetitive tasks like invoice tracking, reconciliations, and generating reports. Now, with automation and built-in workflows, those tasks are much faster and less prone to errors.
It also solves the problem of fragmented data. With Sage Intacct, all financial information is centralized and easy to access, which means I don’t have to dig through multiple systems or spreadsheets to find what I need. That’s a huge time-saver and reduces the risk of mistakes.
The reporting and dashboard features are another big benefit. I can quickly generate customized reports and get real-time insights into spending, budgets, and vendor activity. That helps me stay on top of deadlines and make better decisions, especially when managing accounts payable.

column7: I use Sage Intacct as the main A/P program in my job and appreciate its automation over the older Sage software version. This automation is significant in reducing the data entry workload, enabling me to upload invoices, which eliminates some of the repetitive tasks associated with my job as an A/P Coordinator. Considering about 80 to 90% of my responsibilities involve posting hundreds of invoices daily, the ability to process them through uploads substantially lightens this massive workload. The transition to Sage Intacct from an earlier Sage version, MAS 200, was smooth as most functions are similar, making it easy to acclimate to, aligned with other A/P software. Overall, its enhancement in automation and straightforward setup are the most valuable aspects to me.
column8: I find the invoice upload feature problematic because it often misreads many of my invoices. Many invoices have multiple backups attached, and Sage Intacct tends to read the information from the backups instead of the actual invoice. This issue means I frequently have to manually edit and adjust the information when the posting is in draft form, adding details that cannot automatically be deduced from the invoice itself.

column7: I love Sage Intacct’s reporting capabilities. It has dramatically reduced the reporting period by minimizing the reliance on Excel spreadsheets. Additionally, managers can view their customized reports directly on their dashboards without needing me to send them via Excel, allowing for a tailored and efficient experience. The platform’s ability to operate seamlessly in a remote environment is invaluable as it allows transactions, vendor bill approvals, and expense management to be handled from any location, which streamlines our workflow tremendously. My team is thrilled with Sage Intacct as it offers significantly more capabilities compared to our previous accounting system, Blackbaud Financial Edge.
column8: I dislike that the main Accounts Payable module in Sage Intacct does not allow for entering expenses across multiple departments. This limitation requires us to perform additional steps, like allocating an invoice to a single department and then doing a journal entry for distribution across departments. I find this process cumbersome and not efficient for handling invoices that involve multiple managers or departments. Moreover, there is a lack of functionality for nonprofit organizations to manage endowments directly within the system. This necessitates continued reliance on external sheets, like Excel, to manage assets and their financial details, which feels outdated and inefficient.
column10: I use Sage Intacct for accounts management and it streamlines approvals and transactions remotely. Its reporting capabilities simplify the reporting process, reducing reliance on Excel, and enable customized dashboards for managers. Streamlined Reporting but Needs Departmental

column7: Intacct is a tool that’s easy to access from anywhere and keeps itself up to date automatically. It gives you snapshots of your finances in real time and lets you slice and dice your data by things like project, department, or location. It takes the headache out of manual accounting; automating tasks like billing, reconciliations, and close-outs and handles everything from multi-entity consolidation to different currencies without breaking a sweat.
column8: Sage Intacct can feel like more than some teams bargained for—it’s pricier than simpler tools, and you often need help setting it up and learning the ropes.
column10: I use Sage Intacct to simplify the juggle of grants and restricted funds, and it honestly makes my life easier. I can tie expenses directly to specific grants, flag what’s reimbursable, and really see how budgets are tracking in real time, which cuts down on end-of-quarter panic and audit prep. It keeps everything in one centralized system, so I’m not jumping through spreadsheets or lost in file folders, and I can run reports across grants even when their timelines don’t match our fiscal year

column7:
One of the best advantages of Sage Intacct is its great support for companies with multiple entities. Intacct allows you to set up different locations with each entity which makes it easy manage your books across different entities, including foreign entities. This is also reflected in the custom reporting options that you can obtain by customizing various reports depending on your organization’s needs.
Additionally, the ability to assign departments to specific accounts gives you better visibility and control over departmental performance. Revenue scheduling is another strong feature as it lets you automate and manage complex revenue recognition scenarios with ease. The fixed asset module is detailed and thorough, enabling accurate tracking, depreciation, and reporting of assets over their lifecycle.

Sage Intacct also has a robust billing system that integrates with other software, such as Sales Force, which allows our accounting managers follow the billing process from invoice creation to revenue recognition. There is also support for deferred billing and revenue schedules. The same also applies to the AP side. We can use the Intacct AP module or use a third-party and get the needed results and visibility.
column8: The software can be slow at times, especially when handling large data sets or running complex reports. I noticed that new updates occasionally introduce bugs or issues, which can disrupt our workflows. The user interface (UI) feels outdated in my opinion compared to more modern platforms, and there’s room for improvement.
column10: Sage Intacct helps our organization by streamlining multi-entity accounting, automating revenue recognition, and improving reporting visibility. also, when I've had problems, the customer service team has helped me very quickly.

column10:
Honestly, Intacct makes my work a lot smoother. Before, I had to deal with a bunch of manual tasks—tracking invoices, chasing approvals, and trying to keep everything organized across different systems. Now, most of that is automated or at least way easier to manage.

column7:
What I like is how customizable the system is, which makes it easy to start and implement. The dashboard is clean and gives you a great snapshot of financials . I especially like how you can tailor it to show exactly what matters to your role or department.

The automation features are also a huge win. Routine tasks like billing, and journal entries are streamlined, which saves us hours every week. The multi-entity and multi-currency capabilities are another game-changer as it makes managing multiple companies under one system so much easier and far less error-prone than with other platforms I've used.

One more plus is the real-time reporting. You don’t have to wait for end-of-month to get visibility into performance. Being able to generate custom reports with just a few clicks is incredibly helpful, especially for quick decision-making. The application can be used 24/7 whenever we need it, any time is perfect.

The integration with other systems is smooth, and their support team has always been responsive whenever we’ve needed help.
column8:
One thing I dislike about Sage Intacct is that the user interface can feel a bit outdated and unintuitive, especially for new users. Some of the navigation isn’t very streamlined, and it can take extra time to find what you need or complete tasks that should be simple. Also, certain customizations and reporting features, while powerful, often require advanced knowledge or support to use effectively. It’s a solid system overall, but definitely has a learning curve and could benefit from a more modern, user-friendly design.
I would love if some short tutorials were available and the Search Function could use a boost, The search feature gets the job done—if you know exactly what you're looking for. But if you’re even slightly off, it acts like the record doesn’t exist.
column10:
For one, it centralizes all of our financial data, so instead of juggling spreadsheets or disconnected systems, we have a single source of truth for accounting, reporting. This has drastically improved accuracy and reduced manual errors.

The automation of recurring tasks—like journal entries, invoicing, and revenue recognition—has saved us a ton of time and freed up our team to focus on more strategic work instead of repetitive admin tasks.

One of the biggest benefits is also the real-time reporting and dashboards. Real live feedback from Sage

column7: I've been using Sage Intacct primarily for integration tasks and appreciate its interface, once I became familiar with it. The system's provision of an API that allows me to write integrations without needing custom solutions, utilizing classic REST APIs, is extremely beneficial. I find the documentation regarding the REST API quite helpful, even though I would like it to include a full schema for easier model implementation. Sage Intacct's General Ledger module is particularly enjoyable to work with, providing a rewarding experience during my usage. Moreover, creating a Sage app and authenticating within Sage Intacct's ecosystem for API access has been notably useful. The GL Journal entry integration stands out, particularly because I can specify all the fields needed. Recently added example values have further enhanced my ability to work effectively. Finally, I appreciate the continued development and improvements in the REST API and its documentation, which make me inclined to recommend Sage Intacct to others.
column8: I found the initial setup of Sage Intacct challenging, particularly due to the confusing user interface. Having such a schema readily available would greatly enhance usability and efficiency in creating integration models.

column8:
While Sage Intacct is a powerful accounting system overall, there are a few areas that could be improved. The user interface feels a bit outdated and not as intuitive as some modern cloud platforms, so it can take new users some time to get comfortable. Reporting is flexible, but building or customizing reports can be tricky without some technical knowledge. I’ve also noticed that performance sometimes slows down when working with large data sets or complex dimensions. Lastly, their customer support can be hit or miss — response times vary depending on the issue.
Overall, it’s a solid system once you get used to it, but there’s definitely room for improvement in usability and support.
column10: Sage Intacct has made our accounting and financial processes much more efficient by automating tasks such as billing, reporting, and consolidations. column7: What I like best about Sage Intacct is the way it combines strong financial management capabilities with automation and real-time reporting. The cloud-based system makes it easy to access information anytime, anywhere, and the dimensional reporting feature is especially powerful because it allows me to slice and analyze data across departments, projects, or locations without building complex spreadsheets. It really helps reduce manual work, improves accuracy, and gives management the insights they need to make faster, better decisions.
column8: One area I find challenging with Sage Intacct is that the system can feel a bit complex for new users, especially when navigating through multiple modules or setting up custom reports. There’s a learning curve in the beginning, and sometimes even small configuration changes may require additional support. However, once you get familiar with it, the flexibility and reporting capabilities far outweigh that initial difficulty.
column10: The benefit for me is that it saves a significant amount of time, reduces errors, and gives me more confidence in the accuracy of financial data. It also allows me to focus less on transactional work and more on analysis and decision support, which adds real value to the organization Good

column7:
Sage Intacct is a robust cloud-based financial management solution that offers a wide range of user-friendly processes designed to simplify complex accounting tasks. One of its standout features is the intuitive user interface, which allows even non-financial users to quickly navigate through the system and execute tasks efficiently. The dashboard is highly customizable, providing users with real-time insights into key financial data, enabling better decision-making.

The solution supports multiple applications across various industries, making it highly versatile. Sage Intacct seamlessly integrates with a range of third-party tools, including Salesforce, ADP, and other enterprise systems, ensuring a smooth flow of data across different business functions. Its multi-entity and multi-currency capabilities make it an excellent choice for organizations with complex global operations. Users can easily manage multiple subsidiaries, currencies, and tax structures from a single platform, reducing the need for separate software solutions.

Moreover, Sage Intacct’s automation features help streamline workflows, reducing manual data entry and increasing accuracy. Users can automate routine tasks like invoicing, revenue recognition, and reporting, allowing finance teams to focus on more strategic initiatives. The solution also provides advanced analytics, offering deep insights into financial performance, which is crucial for businesses looking to optimize their financial operations.

In conclusion, Sage Intacct’s combination of user-friendly processes, flexibility, and powerful applications makes it an ideal solution for businesses of all sizes looking to modernize their financial management and improve operational efficiency.
column8: While Sage Intacct offers many advantages, there are some drawbacks to consider. First, the platform may have a steep learning curve for users without prior accounting software experience. Despite its user-friendly interface, mastering the advanced features and workflows can require significant training time. Additionally, its pricing can be higher than some competing solutions, especially for smaller businesses, as it’s generally priced based on usage, modules, and customization options. Another potential disadvantage is that while Sage Intacct offers strong integrations, setting up these integrations with third-party applications can sometimes be complex and require additional IT support. Finally, although Sage Intacct is powerful, smaller organizations may find it overkill for their simpler needs, as it’s primarily designed for mid-market and larger enterprises.

column7: As an Accounting Manager in the nonprofit sector overseeing multiple staff and complex fund/grant tracking, Sage Intacct has been a game changer. The multi-entity capabilities are excellent, and the ability to tag transactions by dimensions (location, project, department, etc.) gives us clean reporting across properties and funding streams. Dashboards and performance cards allow me to monitor AR/AP, grants, and monthly close tasks in real time—super useful for managing a team. I also appreciate the automation tools like checklists, recurring JE templates, and smart rules/triggers that we use to keep processes tight. The fact that it integrates with ADP and has strong compliance/reporting features (e.g., SEFA prep, 401(k) oversight, etc.) has saved us a ton of time.
column8: The user interface feels clunky at times, especially for newer staff. Navigation is not always intuitive, and too many clicks are required for common tasks. I’d also love to see better bulk editing options for things like vendor updates or report group management. Some features (like checklist audit trails and role-based dashboard controls) are more rigid than they should be. Also, while reporting is powerful, it can take a while to build exactly what you need unless you’re very experienced.
column10: Sage Intacct has solved a number of pain points we used to face as a growing nonprofit managing complex funding sources, property acquisitions, and multi-department operations. It has drastically improved our ability to track and report on restricted vs. unrestricted funds, grants, HUD contracts, and property-specific financials—all in one place without relying on spreadsheets. The dimensional accounting allows us to easily slice financials by project, funder, and location, which is crucial for audit prep and board reporting. It’s also helped streamline our monthly close with tools like checklist templates, role-based dashboards, and recurring entries. As a manager, I benefit from real-time visibility into my team’s workload and deadlines, which helps me support them without micromanaging. column7:
I like that Sage Intacct really automates a lot of the accounting work I’d normally have to do manually. Things like invoicing, tracking payments, and closing out the books are much faster and way less stressful.

The reporting and dashboards are also a big plus. I can pull up real-time numbers, break things down by client, project, or location, and get a clear picture of how things are performing without digging through spreadsheets.

Another thing I like is how scalable and flexible it is. It handles multiple entities and currencies easily, and it integrates well with other tools, which saves a ton of time switching between systems.

And since it’s cloud-based, I can access everything from anywhere, which makes it easier to manage projects and clients on the go.
column8:
One thing I don’t love about Sage Intacct is that it can feel a little complicated at first. There are a lot of features and settings, so the learning curve can be steep if you’re new to it.

Some of the customizations also take extra time and effort — it’s powerful, but not always the most straightforward to set up exactly how you want it.

And while the reporting is great, creating really specific, advanced reports sometimes requires more steps than I’d like.
column10:
Sage Intacct helps streamline a lot of the messy parts of accounting and financial management. It cuts down on manual work like tracking invoices, payments, and expenses, which saves me a ton of time.

It also makes it easier to manage multiple clients and projects at once. I can quickly see real-time numbers, compare performance across locations, and get accurate reports without digging through spreadsheets.

Because everything is in one place and cloud-based, it’s easier to stay organized, make faster decisions, and keep things running smoothly.

column7:
Sage Intacct has been a huge upgrade for us (we used to use Sage 300).

I love how automated things have become - things like invoices, payments, and expense tracking basically run themselves now. The reports and dashboards are super clear, and it is easy to get both the big picture and the fine details when we need them.
My personal favourite is that you put the banking information in the same stop as the vendor information. I know it's a small detail, but it saves me so much time when a vendor updates their information.
column8:
The one feature I feel it could benefit from is having it's own payroll program - I really enjoyed that feature in Sage 300 and will miss it. Especially with the price increase.

I also think there should be a way to use the same cheque number if an error is made. When I first started using the system I accidentally posted a cheque payment through top level. I reversed it in the top level and posted in at the entity level, but it wouldn't let me reuse the cheque number (even after I went in and changed the cheque sequence number). I get that it's a safety feature, but as a human we all make mistakes and it would be nice to have the option to fix it.

It would be nice for future customers that are making the switch to be able to pull information from Sage 300 (if that's they system they are leaving). It took me weeks to make spreadsheets of all our AP & AR vendors (names, addresses, phone, banking info, etc.). We are a small company so I can't imagine how time consuming it would be for a large entity to make the switch.
column10:
It is making reporting so much easier. In our previous system anytime we received new funding we would have to make all new codes (travel, honoraria, etc.). This is much easier - one travel code - just link it to the correct fund. 
I also love that I can make AP automated and that I can post-date payments.

column7: The system is very intuitive, and easy to use. We currently only use in single currency, but have multiple entities that roll into a top level. column7: Sage Intacct has allowed us to streamline almost every account process we had to do in other software systems. It has eliminated the need for paper and filing cabinets: automated Company credit card systems, ACH Payments, and Automated Budgeting through integration with Martus. During COVID, we would not have all been able to work remotely in Finance without Sage Intacct. The bank reconciliation process is complete within 5 days of the month's close. We create a robust Purchase Order process with multiple levels of approvals. As the CFO & COO of Intentional Life Media dba Family Life Radio & Intentional Living, I would recommend Sage Intacct to anyone struggling with an outdated accounting solution.

column7:
The intercompany features for multiple entities run really smooth and allow for easy reconciliations.
The creation rules on the banking saves ample time each month.
The ability to edit and delete journals within the open accounting period keeps the ledgers looking tidy.
The Dashboard functionality gives our budget holders a live feed of their finances which saves us time in producing reports and they then have access to their data whenever they need it.
The Sage university for the training was really helpful during the implementation process.
column8:
Some of the bank posting types don't make sense such as we can't post a supplier refund directly to the supplier account we had to put together a work-around.
The finance report functions take a bit of getting used to and are a bit complicated, which is fine if you use reporting all the time but if you don't it can be hard to follow.
Many of the add on software companies are USA based and not available elsewhere which is a shame as their integration with the system would really improve our processes.
column10: Sage intacct has brought our software into the 21st century, it has allowed for faster month end processes & quicker, more accurate access to data for our budget holders. A+ for effort

column7: We use Sage Intacct for all our accounting and finance needs. We love that it is in the cloud, and all of our division leaders have their own custom dashboards so they can monitor their revenue and expenses in real time. This allows them to make better and informed decisions about their budgets. They have access to their data 24 hours a day. We also use it for all invoice approvals and allows us to have a record of those approvals. All of our reports come directly out of the system, so there is no need to use Excel or other software to manipulate reporting.
column8:

column10:
The availability of data in real-time has helped with better decision making
Instead of dealing with paper invoices, we can get department head approvals electronically
We were able to get rid of an on-site server for the old accounting system - glad to be in the cloud

column7: One new feature I really appreciate in Sage Intacct is the enhanced user interface and navigation updates. They have made it quicker and more intuitive to get where I need to go. I also like the continued improvements in reporting flexibility, especially with custom report building and dimension-based filtering. These updates have made it easier to get meaningful insights without needing to export to Excel. The increased automation around approvals and workflows has also helped streamline our processes.
column8: One area that makes me like Sage Intacct less is that some processes still feel a bit clunky or unintuitive, especially for newer users. For example, navigating between modules can require more clicks than expected, and some reports still require manual adjustments or exporting to Excel to get exactly what I need. I’ve also experienced occasional issues with slow load times, and certain integrations or customizations can be more complex than expected without support. Overall, it's powerful but there's still room for improvement in usability and streamlining routine tasks.

column7: Sage Intacct is easy to use and intuitive, especially for time entry. Our team appreciates how straightforward it is to log hours and track them by project and role. The clean interface and logical flow make it simple for everyone to stay on top of their entries without needing much training.
column8: There are too many fields available when entering time or project data, which can feel overwhelming. While flexibility is great, it would be helpful if the system allowed for customization to reduce the number of required or visible fields based on role or use case. Streamlining this could improve efficiency and reduce user error.
column10: Sage Intacct helps solve a key operational challenge for our team: tracking time accurately across multiple projects and roles. By streamlining time entry, it ensures that hours are logged consistently, which supports better project budgeting, resource planning, and reporting. Its intuitive interface makes it easy for team members to use with minimal training, reducing administrative overhead. This has led to more reliable data and improved visibility into how engineering hours are allocated—ultimately helping us make more informed decisions and stay on top of project performance.

column7:
Sage Intacct has been an excellent solution for our business, especially when managing multiple entities. The intercompany support is a standout feature, making it seamless to handle transactions and reporting across different companies.

The platform is also easy to navigate, with an intuitive interface that shortens the learning curve for new users. Building and customizing reports is straightforward, and the reporting tools are powerful—providing clear, accurate insights without unnecessary complexity.

Overall, Sage Intacct has streamlined our financial processes and given us reliable visibility across the organization. It’s a strong choice for businesses looking for efficiency and scalability

Customer Support is always helpful and quick to respond.
column8: Accounts Receivable (AR) functionality leaves room for improvement. We currently rely on an outside POS system, and Intacct does not allow us to attach PDFs directly to invoices and send them to customers. As a result, much of our AR follow-up has to be handled outside the platform, which creates extra steps in the process.

column8: While the data is all there, it takes a considerable amount of customization to set it up. I run a professional services company, and it took us a full 9 months to finish implementation, which never did get done correctly. We don't a work-in-progress report that works properly, we don't have automated invoicing templates, we don't have dashboards set up, and the list could go on. On top of that, there's essentially no customer service from Sage directly. They can't help troubleshoot any issues and offer no support. You must do everything internally or with an implementation consultant.

column7:
Alright, buckle up, because Sage Intacct absolutely deserves this glowing review!

From the get-go, this cloud accounting software has been a game-changer for our business. We were drowning in spreadsheets and outdated systems, and Intacct swooped in like a superhero with a cape made of efficiency.

The real-time visibility it offers is incredible. We can now see exactly where we stand financially, drilling down into the details with ease. No more waiting for month-end reports that feel like ancient history! The customizable dashboards are fantastic – we've tailored them to show us the key metrics that matter most, making informed decisions a breeze.

What really sets Intacct apart is its robustness and scalability. As we've grown, it's grown with us, handling increasing complexity without a hiccup. The multi-dimensional reporting is a lifesaver, allowing us to analyze our data in ways we never thought possible. Seriously, slicing and dicing financial information has become… dare I say… enjoyable?

And let's talk integrations. Intacct plays nicely with so many other crucial business systems. This seamless connectivity has streamlined our workflows and eliminated so much manual data entry. It's like all our essential tools are finally speaking the same language.

Finally, the support team is top-notch. Whenever we've had a question (and let's be honest, with any new system there are always a few!), they've been responsive, knowledgeable, and genuinely helpful. It feels like they're true partners in our success.

Honestly, if you're looking for a powerful, flexible, and future-proof accounting solution, look no further than Sage Intacct. It's not just software; it's a strategic asset that has significantly improved our financial operations. Five stars all the way! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
column8:
Okay, so while I'm a big fan, no system is perfect, right? If I had to nitpick something someone could dislike about Sage Intacct, it might be the initial learning curve and implementation.

Because it's such a robust and feature-rich platform, getting everything set up exactly how you want it and training your team can take a bit more time and effort upfront compared to simpler, less comprehensive solutions. It's not necessarily difficult, but it does require a commitment to the process. If a business is looking for something incredibly basic right out of the box with minimal setup, the depth of Intacct might feel a little overwhelming initially.
column10:
Basically, it kicks a lot of the annoying parts of my job to the curb. Think mountains of invoices to type in? Intacct often grabs that info automatically – huge time-saver and way fewer typos. Chasing down who needs to approve what? It routes invoices electronically and pings people, so stuff doesn't get stuck on someone's desk for ages.

I can actually see what's going on with our bills in real-time, not just some dusty report at the end of the month. Need to know if we paid a certain vendor? Boom, it's right there. Got different parts of the company? Intacct keeps all those bills straight in one place, which is a lifesaver. Plus, it's got my back on catching mistakes like paying the same bill twice – that's always a good thing!

So, instead of drowning in paperwork and playing detective, I can actually get stuff done faster and more accurately. It makes my day way less chaotic and honestly, makes me feel like I'm actually contributing instead of just pushing paper. It's a solid tool that makes my AP life much easier.

column7: Sage Intacct has been a game-changer for our accounting operations, offering an impressive combination of ease of use, user-friendliness, and powerful features. The intuitive interface makes navigating the system simple, even for those new to the platform, while its extensive functionality—ranging from multi-entity consolidation to automated workflows—has helped streamline our processes and improve efficiency. What sets Sage Intacct apart is not just its robust feature set, but also its exceptional customer service; the support team is always responsive, knowledgeable, and willing to go the extra mile to help. From seamless integrations with tools like Salesforce to real-time financial visibility, Sage Intacct has exceeded our expectations and significantly enhanced how we manage our financials.
column8: While Sage Intacct has greatly improved our financial operations with its ease of use, user-friendly interface, and rich set of features, there are a few areas where it could improve. The initial setup and customization can be a bit complex and may require assistance from a consultant or support, especially for companies with more intricate accounting needs. Additionally, while the reporting capabilities are powerful, creating highly customized reports can involve a bit of a learning curve. That said, these are relatively minor trade-offs considering the overall value and functionality the platform provides.
column10: Sage Intacct is solving several key problems for our organization, particularly around financial visibility, efficiency, and scalability. Before switching, we struggled with manual processes, limited reporting capabilities, and managing financials across multiple entities. Sage Intacct has addressed these challenges by automating many routine accounting tasks—such as approvals, journal entries, and consolidations—which has significantly reduced errors and saved time. The multi-entity and multi-currency support has made it much easier to manage our growing operations globally. Most importantly, the real-time reporting and customizable dashboards give us immediate insight into our financial performance, enabling better, faster decision-making. Overall, Sage Intacct has improved our accuracy, efficiency, and ability to scale, which has had a direct positive impact on our business operations.

column7: What I really like about Sage Intacct is that it takes a lot of the heavy lifting out of managing our financial processes. Things that used to take hours—like generating reports or handling payables—are now mostly automated, which has freed up a lot of time for our team to focus on analysis instead of data entry. The accounts payable automation, in particular, has been a game-changer for us. I also really appreciate how the dashboards are customizable. I’ve set mine up to highlight the KPIs and trends that matter most to our team, and it’s helped us spot issues or opportunities much faster than before. Since it’s cloud-based, it’s easy to log in from anywhere, which has made collaboration with other departments way smoother—especially with some of us working remotely or across different locations. It’s not just a tool we use—it’s become part of how we operate.
column8: Like with any robust system, there’s a learning curve. Some of the modules can feel a bit complex at first, especially if you’re coming from simpler tools like QuickBooks. Also, occasional lags or syncing delays have happened, but they’re rare.
column10: Before Sage Intacct, we were struggling with manual data entry, reconciling data across different spreadsheets, and limited reporting capabilities. Intacct has centralized everything—now we have accurate, real-time financial data, better audit trails, and more reliable forecasting. It’s helped us streamline accounting and focus more on strategic planning rather than just data crunching.

column7: Sage Intacct stands out for its robust financial reporting and automation capabilities. column8: While Sage Intacct is powerful, the user interface can feel unintuitive for new users, requiring a steep learning curve. column10: Sage Intacct is helping solve the problem of manual, time-consuming invoice processing and tracking. Finally, it is just amazing that Sage hasn't brokered agreements to work with all the major banks at least here in the United States. column7: What I like best about Sage Intacct is its powerful and user-friendly financial management features that make it easy to gain real-time visibility into financial data. The customizable dashboards and robust reporting tools allow for quick, insightful decision-making, while the cloud-based system ensures accessibility from anywhere. Its seamless integration capabilities with other business applications also make processes more efficient and reduce manual data entry. Overall, Sage Intacct simplifies complex accounting tasks and helps streamline financial operations, which saves both time and resources.
column8: What I don't like about Sage Intacct is that the user interface can feel a bit dated and unintuitive at times, especially for new users who are still learning the system. Some processes require multiple steps that could be streamlined, and certain customizations or reports can be difficult to set up without technical assistance. Additionally, while the system is powerful, the learning curve can be steep, and the help documentation isn’t always as clear or comprehensive as it could be. These factors can slow down onboarding and day-to-day use for teams not already familiar with the platform.
column10: Sage Intacct is solving several key problems related to financial management, especially around inefficiencies, lack of visibility, and manual processes. It helps eliminate time-consuming tasks like data entry and spreadsheet-based reporting by automating core accounting functions such as accounts payable, receivable, and general ledger. One of the biggest benefits is real-time visibility into financial data through customizable dashboards and reports, which makes it easier to track performance, manage budgets, and make informed decisions quickly. It also supports multi-entity and multi-currency consolidation, which is a huge plus for organizations operating in different regions. Overall, Sage Intacct is helping streamline workflows, reduce errors, and improve accuracy, saving both time and money. SAGE

column7: I love that I can do multiple things for one invoice in one system. I have to use Intacct every day.
column8: I dislike how long it initially takes to get to the needed pages. Having to open multiple tabs makes searching easier and cuts down on the amount of time I spend waiting for the page to load. It would be easier for me if everything I needed to do with one invoice could be done from the same page. I frequently have to refund invoices, and this can't be done from Intacct. I have to go to Intacct to find the information and take it to Braintree (both EFT and CC versions). There's about a week's waiting period for everything to process and sync; then I have to use Intacct to verse on one page and reprocess on another. It isn't very easy to learn at first either. Since there are so many back and forth steps, a lot of manual fixes, one thing at a time, it takes a lot of patience and asking questions. column7:
Sage has honestly made a lot of things easier for us. One of the biggest improvements is being able to go back and update journal entries without having to create an separate adjusting journal entry. That alone saves time and keeps everything cleaner.

It also takes care of intercompany transactions automatically, which used to be a headache we had to do manually. That's been a huge help.

Another big plus is that it connects directly to Stampli, which makes processing invoices way more efficient. Everything syncs up really well, and we don't have to double-handle anything. 

Compared to the old version of Dynamics we were using, the interface is a lot more modern and easier to work with. Reports are easier to customize, and since it's cloud based, I have been able to transition to work from home! Overall, it's just made our whole workflow smoother.
column8: The prepaid module is honestly the one area I really do not care for. The way it's set up to enter the information for the prepaid is confusing and frustrating. I've tried it a few times, but it just ends up being more frustrating than helpful. At this point, I just do my prepaid entries manually because it's faster and I know they will be correct.
column10:
Sage Intacct has saved me a ton of time. Before, things like intercompany transactions had to be entered manually, which could get messy fast. Now it handles those automatically and I don't have to second guess if I missed something.

It's also made updates way easier, I can fix journal entries without needing to run a whole adjusting entry, which makes everything cleaner. On top of that, it connects to Stampli, so invoices flow through a lot smoother than they used to.

Overall, it's taken a lot of repetitive, annoying stuff off of my plate so I can focus on actual work instead of fixing problems caused by outdated software. Big Improvements After Leaving Legacy

column10: Sage Intacct has been a great upgrade for handling more complex accounting needs. When I worked at Exploding Kittens, we outgrew QuickBooks because it just couldn’t keep up with our financial operations. We needed better multi-entity management, automation, and reporting tools—and that’s where Sage Intacct delivered. It made bank reconciliations, revenue tracking, and reporting much smoother while cutting down on manual work. The built-in audit trails and approval workflows also helped improve accuracy and compliance. For any company scaling up, Sage Intacct is a solid choice!

column8:
Too many options for processes; can take too many steps for small businesses.
I'm glad you are delaying, hopefully reversing, the decision to do away with the "receive a payment" option in AR. This is the one place where I can easily see everything open on an account, so even though it can unreasonably refuse to accept an entry (after correcting) or not always show all invoices, I use it almost exclusively to enter customer payments and look up what's outstanding.
Trying to enter a customer refund is cumbersome. That or I haven't been able to find a straightforward way to do this. Currently, I enter a debit adjustment to reverse the credit memo, then apply it. Then go to Cash Management/Manual Payment and enter the refund amount - as cash for tracking- only so it doesn't issue a check or CC entry. This often requires setup of a customer as an AP vendor, making the vendor list a jumble of AP vendors and customers.
Recently you changed the reference on overpayments/payments to customer accounts, to a random sequenced C number on the AR Aging Report. Why? I used to be able to easily figure out exactly what (check #) the credit resulted from because it labelled the credit as such. Now I have to go into another screen - yet another step.

Just some thoughts from an amateur.

column7: Sage Intacct is great for developers because of its solid API and easy integration options. The API is well-documented and structured, making it simple to connect with other apps and automate tasks. Pulling data and customizing reports is a breeze, which is super helpful for building custom financial workflows. Since it’s cloud-based, it’s scalable and gives real-time access to data, making development more efficient.
column8: Some features require workarounds, and the documentation, while detailed, can sometimes be unclear or lack real-world examples. Debugging API issues can also be tricky since error messages aren’t always specific enough, and there aren’t enough logs to easily track issues. Additionally, the UI for admin tasks and setup isn’t the most intuitive, which can slow down configuration and testing. While it’s a strong platform overall, there’s room for improvement in making development and integration smoother.
column10: With the recent launch of its REST API, Sage Intacct has made integrations way easier and more modern. It simplifies data exchange, boosts automation, and makes building scalable financial solutions smoother. Developers no longer have to rely on outdated workarounds, and reporting, multi-entity management, and overall workflow automation are much more efficient now. Powerful but Could Be More

column8:
1. You never know what's not going to work next. Every month Sage has a new issue when trying to produce AIA style billings.
2. They claim there is one standard cost code for all jobs, this is beyond a lie. Every single job in the system has its own set of cost codes that you have to manage. We have around 400 actual cost codes but have to manage over 800,000 thanks to how they have this set up. 
3. The GL interface is extremely clunky and slow for user input. They don't allow you to copy the job or the entity down so you have to input on every transaction line. Their default setting doesn't work unless ever line item is going to the same project because every job has it's own set of cost codes.
4. Researching in Intacct is slow and difficult, if you've used Sage 100 in the past this will be a huge down grade
5. The Interactive Custom Reports are a joke. Who designs a report tool that you cannot display what information was pulled in the report? What job is this, what time period, any cost codes excluded? You'll never know once you hit print.
6. Naming conventions are not consistent; there is no consistency between the names used on the Object list and what is used in the Custom reports which makes trying to build out your own reports difficult. Which is a major issue as the system comes with no stock reports.
column10: Causes lots of problems, doesn't really solve any. Avoid

column7:
As someone handling purchasing, operations, & account management, and using Sage Intacct on a daily basis, what I like most about Sage Intacct is how it simplifies financial processes and keeps everything organized in real time. Instead of juggling multiple spreadsheets or manually tracking orders and expenses, everything is centralized, making it much easier to manage purchasing workflows, financial records, and client accounts efficiently. 

For purchasing, it is helpful to have a clear, real-time view of vendor transactions, expenses, and order history without having to dig through emails or paper records. The ability to track inventory, costs, and past purchases ensures nothing gets overlooked, helping to maintain accurate records and avoid duplicate or unnecessary orders. This is especially useful when working with multiple vendors or managing high-volume procurement, as I can quickly pull up details on pricing, past orders, or payment history without delays. 

Another key advantage is Sage Intacct is cloud-based, and I do not have to be at a specific location to check on purchasing, financials, or order statuses. Whether I am in the office, working remotely, or on the go, I can easily access real-time data and keep things moving. This is particularly beneficial when urgent orders come up and need to be addressed quickly. 

On the operations side, the software helps with budget tracking, cost analysis, and overall financial visibility. I can generate detailed reports to see spending trends, identify cost-saving opportunities, and make better purchasing decisions. The ability to integrate with other systems, such as inventory management or CRM software, further streamlines operations by ensuring purchasing, finance, and sales teams are always working with the same up-to-date information. 

In account management, Sage Intacct makes it easy to track client orders, billing, and revenue streams. Having all financial data in one place ensures invoices are accurate, payments are processed correctly, and client accounts are up to date. If a client has a question about an order or invoice, I can quickly access the necessary details and resolve issues efficiently, providing accurate and timely customer support.

Overall, what I like most is how organized, user-friendly, and efficient Sage Intacct is for managing purchasing, order entry, and client accounts. It saves time, reduces errors, and ensures smooth daily operations, making it an essential tool for staying on top of operational processes.
column8:
My biggest frustration with Sage Intacct is how poorly it integrates with QStock and certain aspects of Salesforce. Since both are widely used inventory and sales management systems, I expected a seamless connection, but instead, integrations are unreliable. 

There are frequent discrepancies for item quantities on order, on hand, on hold, & available where data between the two systems does not match up, causing discrepancies between purchase orders, inventory counts, and customer orders. This often means our teams have to manually double-check data, which completely defeats the purpose of having an integrated, automated system. 

Another issue is updates between Sage Intacct and QStock don’t always push through properly or in a timely manner, which can lead to delays in tracking received goods, verifying stock levels, and processing customer orders. This makes it harder to ensure accurate purchasing decisions because I cannot fully trust the inventory data is correct. 

It also lacks seamless two-way communication, meaning I sometimes have to re-enter or adjust information in both Salesforce & Intacct systems manually. This not only wastes time but also increases the risk of errors in customer account records. 

If Sage Intacct had a more reliable integration with QStock and Salesforce, it would make purchasing and inventory management much smoother. Right now, the inefficiencies force unnecessary workarounds, which is frustrating when trying to maintain accuracy and efficiency in operations.

Lastly, it is frustrating that some fields are unavailable to add as columns in personalized views.
column10: Sage Intacct benefits me by streamlining daily tasks, reducing manual work, and providing real-time insights, which in turn boosts efficiency, accuracy, and the ability to make informed decisions quickly. Sage Intacct Simplifies Purchasing and Operations for

column8:
The consolidation module. In large group scenario when using the same COA across the group and having multi currencies, there is no way to hold each of the historical FX transactions at a specific rate given you only have one GL account for Share Capital etc. 
The consolidation module converts P/L transactions at a monthly avg rate but for a UK GAAP consol the P/L in foreign currencies should be converted at a yearly avg rate, there is no easy work around for this. 
There is no easy way to export FX rates out of Sage.
