column7: Sage Intacct was a breath of fresh air after other mid-size ERPs I have experienced inthe past. So user-friendly, easy to learn and understand to start taking advantage of all the features right away. It allows for what I call 3D accounting, where any GL number can be combined with any department or other dimension you chose to track. Which in turn provides an opportunity to have a much shorter COA. At the same time, Sage Intacct is robust enought to allow set up of smart rules, where you can restrict certain GL, Dept, Station combination to not have advertising expense in Engineering and insurance expense in Programing, for example. Reporting is great as well: flexible and easy to use for regular, non-IT oriented users to create reports. The system is very flexible to accommodate various business needs like reporting on mulptipe markets and radio station within them, setting up various books to allow for posting of non-cash (barter) transactions in a separate set of books, while also allowing seamless consolidated reporting on both. It seems the sky is the limit with Sage Intacct.
column8: It is not a dislike list, more of the wish list for ptential furure upgrades. For example, an automated JE balancing option wth a write-off of rounding variance between debits and credits to a selected GL account at a selected threshhold would be amazing. A more clear error code that actually gies you the inofmation about the error you need to get it fixed, would be great too. There is the option, where a detaild error report is sent you via email, and just the error code is provided in the JE upload status. To have short error description right there in intacct without having to wait for the email, would be really great.

column7: Sage Intacct is an awesome cloud-based accounting solution that makes managing finances super easy. It’s great for growing businesses because it’s flexible, automates a ton of tasks, and gives you real-time insights into your numbers. I wasn't here for implementation but it seems like it was fairly simple with the help of the folks who onboarded the team. The reporting features are top-notch, and it integrates smoothly with other tools, so everything stays connected. Plus, it saves tons of time by cutting out manual work and reducing errors. With strong security and compliance features, it’s a solid choice for businesses that want better financial control without the hassle. If you’re looking for an easy-to-use, powerful accounting system, Sage Intacct is definitely worth checking out!
column8: At this point, I really don't have any complaints about my time using the platform. Like any new tool, it took me a little while to get used to... But that's normal and definitely not a negative. It's simply a fully built out tool with lots to learn.
column10: Sage Intacct helps solve a bunch of accounting headaches, like manual data entry, slow reporting, and disconnected financial systems. It automates key processes, giving real-time insights into finances, which means way less time spent on spreadsheets and more time making smart business decisions. The ability to integrate with other tools keeps everything streamlined, reducing errors and making month-end closes way faster. Overall, it makes financial management smoother, saves time, and helps businesses grow without the usual accounting struggles. Sage Intacct Makes

column7:
When I first started using Sage Intacct, I was impressed by how intuitive the platform felt. It was easy to start managing invoices, payments, and reconciliations. The system is also very customizable and allows you to tailor workflows to fit unique approval processes. You can even set up custom fields that made our reporting much more meaningful.

Integrating Sage Intacct with CRM and payment systems was surprisingly simple, which traditionally is not the case. This eliminated a lot of manual data entry and reduced errors. Performance and reliablity of the platform was good, there were no downtimes to speak of.
column8:
At times, if I needed to fix a transaction, the process felt a bit clunky due to requiring extra steps.

Creating more advanced, customized reports has a definite learning curve. I needed to dig into the docs to get the insights I needed. It would be nice to have GenBI tools to automatically generate reports based on NLP.

column7: There is really efficient design in just even the basic package of Sage Intacct. The automations in the AP module and in the General Ledger can save so much time and energy. There are really intelligent rules that can be created in the Cash Managment module that save so much time and basically automate what used to be a very manual arduous bank reconciliation. Sage Intacct has a real presence in the market and so when we are using a third party service for say, Payroll, they are familiar with the product and provide in most cases a direct API or at a minimum an easy import. I really appreciate the ease of import in general with Sage Intacct - and it goes for almost everything, JEs, Bills, Bank Transactions. Any time we have had trouble getting an import template to work, the customer service team has been so quick to get back to us with helpful, detailed answers. The customer support team is so great to work with and helpful, and it brings a lot of peace of mind given how critical this system is and the fact we're using it daily.
column8: Admittedly, I was not on the team for implementation, so I can't speak as much to that. Having come in after implementation, I did need to take some time to learn this system. Unfortunately I found the team that was part of implementation still needed to do the same, as over a year later they were still only using what they needed to get the day to day done. Three years later, we are using almost every feature available to us, and we could have eliminated a full time role with the automations and efficiencies we now have in place. Honestly, you just have to take the time to read and learn how to use this system.
column10: Sage Intacct is saving us time in the AP automations and in the create rules as part of Cash Management, the Bank Transactions import/upload, and in recurring and memorized JEs. Each of these give us the greatest benefit of time back day to day and that accumulates so quickly. It has freed myself and my team up to take on other projects and help out other teams as needed, knowing that all the while the monthly entries, the bank reconciliation, bill pay - none of that will suffer.

column7: Our business has liked that we can write our own accounting reports in Sage Intacct and have great flexibility in how those reports function and look. We are a very unique company that cannot use "out of the box" reports because of how we have to report things. Sage Intacct has allowed us to write all of our necessary reports and keep them housed in Intacct. We no longer have to keep separate reports in Excel that we manually have to manipulate. It took us a minute to understand how to use and write reports but once we got the hang of it, nothing stopped us. We use our reporting every day and if there were things we did not know in the beginning we had a team of experts that helped us navigate the system. It is also nice how well Intacct can import a template or Excel file, that helps us integrate with other programs that we use to make sure our system has all the necessary information for reporting.
column8: Honestly, we do not have a lot of negatives to say about Sage Intacct. If we had to come up with one thing we do not like is maybe the functionality of the dashboards. It would be great if they were a little more flexible and customizable.

column7:
I have been using Intacct for more than 2 years now and I'm extremely impressed by its capabilities. This cloud-based financial software management provides remarkable flexibility and scalability, which aligns perfectly with our expanding business. The interface is designed with ease of use allowing smooth and efficient navigation throughout the system.

The most notable feature is the real-time reporting and automation facilities which reduce manual work and minimize errors. Their customer service is amazing and they are quick with their responses.

The integration with other tools like Avalara, has enhanced our workflow and provides easy access to other departments.

I strongly endorse Intacct to any organisation in search of powerful, trustworthy and looking for financial needs in one stop.

column7: Sage Intacct takes the complication out of correcting mistakes. column7: Sage Intacct has an intuitive, user-friendly interface that simplifies accounting tasks. Even for clients who are not particularly tech-savvy, the software provides a seamless experience. Navigating through different modules like general ledger, accounts payable/receivable, and project accounting is straightforward, which helps save time and reduce the learning curve.
column8: For clients who are new to Sage Intacct or transitioning from simpler accounting systems, the learning curve can be challenging. While the basic functionalities are user-friendly, mastering more advanced features such as custom reporting, integrations, or configuring workflows often requires a deeper understanding of the platform. For me as an accountant, this can be time-consuming, especially when I'm trying to get new users up to speed quickly.
column10: Many businesses still rely on spreadsheets or legacy systems to manage accounting processes, which can lead to errors and inefficiencies. The time spent on manual data entry and reconciliations can be overwhelming.Sage Intacct automates routine accounting tasks such as invoicing, billing, and bank reconciliations. The system’s automation reduces human error and frees up time for more strategic work, which is especially beneficial for me as an accountant. I can focus on higher-level analysis and financial advising, rather than spending time on repetitive administrative tasks.This automation boosts efficiency, reduces errors, and allows me to dedicate more time to providing valuable insights to my clients rather than getting bogged down by manual tasks.

column7:
- For almost all AR related tasks there is an upload file available to upload information in batch which saves a lot of time on daily basis.
- It is easy to navigate around Sage Intacct.
- It has multiple integrations available with other softwares which turns cash posting process smoother and faster.
- There is a Sandbox version available where one can try out any ideas of process improvements without taking any risks.
- The Help Page available for every section that one works on in Sage Intacct is a big time-saver. This allows to narrow down the scope of information provided and saves time on finding exactly what is need in less time.
column8:
- There are some limitations for adjustment allocation to an invoice in different currency. One has to work around the system to allocate adjustment in one currency to an invoice in different currency.
- Allocating manually credit notes raised in Order Entry module to invoices raised in AR module is time consuming. It would be great if the system could automatically match these two off.
column10: Sage Intacct helps me and my team save time on repetitive tasks, we use upload files to upload data in batches. This is also helps minimizing human error while introducing data. As the software is cloud based, it makes my work much easier working remotely.

column7: SAGE is relatively easy to navigate, the system is quick to respond. It was easy for my manager to teach me how to make JEs, generate bills and invoices.
column8:
I wish there was an easier way to filter/keep status of certain items. One thing that really bothers myself and my coworkers is tracking the status of a bill along its approved to posted status. I post the bill for review, it is then approved, then I have to pay it, then that has to be approved again before it is eventually posted. I wish there was a notification box aside from getting lost in my emails letting me know it is ready for the next step. 
-we have also had the issue with SAGE connected and disconnected to our bank accounts causing duplicates we need to ensure are being ignored. 
-I also wish there is a way to automate JEs in the near future, there is a lot of manual entry for reoccurring entries that I strongly believe AI could handle.

column7: Reports and Dashboard.What I like best about Sage Intacct is its robust and scalable cloud-based architecture, which allows for seamless financial management and real-time reporting. Its flexibility to integrate with other systems, customizable dashboards, and strong automation capabilities streamline many complex processes. This enables greater efficiency and insight into financial data, which ultimately helps businesses make more informed decisions.
column8: While Sage Intacct offers many powerful features, one area that could be improved is the limited support for certain currencies, such as the Botswana Pula (BWP), in ACH bank files. Additionally, some users may find the learning curve for more advanced features to be steep initially, especially without proper training. However, these are areas that can be mitigated through custom integrations or with additional resources and support from Sage.
column10:
Sage Intacct is solving several key challenges, particularly in automating financial processes and providing real-time insights. It simplifies complex accounting tasks like multi-entity consolidations, revenue recognition, and managing multiple currencies, which saves significant time and reduces the potential for human error.

The ability to generate real-time financial reports helps in making faster, more informed decisions, which is a huge benefit for financial planning and analysis. Additionally, its scalability allows it to grow with the organization, making it a valuable long-term solution for evolving business needs.

Overall, Sage Intacct has greatly improved efficiency, accuracy, and visibility in financial management, allowing us to focus more on strategic initiatives.

column7: My organization transitioned away from Quickbooks to Sage Intaact in 2024. Like all major software transitions, it took many team hours to complete. That being said, my only regret was not making the transition to Safe Intaact sooner. Versus Quickbooks, Sage Intaact is a far more robust software. Some of my favorite features include maintaining approval governance in system and being able to attach backup documentation to entries. Another nice feature is the dashboard, which non-accounting users find helpful and easy to read and the ability to send reports over email automatically.
column8:
Coming from Quickbooks, Sage is a far more expansive software, and it comes with a steep learning curve - especially since, unlike Quickbooks, Sage will maintain control, meaning that you can't simply "delete" incorrect entries. 

My advice is to just stick with it. A few months in, our team got the hang of it.
column10: Sage Intaact is allowing us to send reports and financial data to non-financial users on a faster basis. Sage is also allowing us to maintain accounting control in system, versus relying on email threads or signatures on documents.

column7:
I absolutely love the integration tools for Sage Intacct. I feel like I outsourced myself! 
 Before using Intacct we had to manually create and import a file for our credit card transactions, we had to manually enter our employee expense reimbursement and had to issue checks for our invoices. With the help of my integration team, transitioning to Intacct was a breeze. We integrated our credit card transactions and our employee expenses to automagically sync. We then added on bill pay and it is so easy (literally, it does it without me), now I'm saving around 4 hours a week

I also love dashboards. I built simple to use dashboards for everyone in our organization that has budget responsibility. They can visit them as frequently as they want/need without my help. 

I will say that I can only imagine that customer support (the actual people) is amazing because I have never had the need to use them. The suport options in the training tools and knowledgebase is so well done that I have solved my own issues. If something did come up I know that the support team will be there ready to assist.

column7: Sage Intacct is a highly effective online application designed for comprehensive data management, encompassing accounting, finance, and more. Sage Intacct can seamlessly integrate with other software and online tools, such as QuickBooks and Xero, and offers API connectivity for enhanced functionality.

column7: Sage is very user friendly. Having the ability to connect the banks allows us to easily and quickly create journal entries and credit card transactions directly from the banking cloud as the transactions come in multiple times a day. The customer support from Sage has been amazing. We have received multiple hours of professional help from a Sage expert which was amazing and helped us a lot as we were new to Sage. Also, having quick access to the help center in the top right corner of the page allows to search for any question we may have about Sage and get an explanation right away. Also, being able to customize our dashboard has allowed us to put some of our major financial reports in one, easy-to-see location for the decision makers in our company.
column8: It is very different from the typical, basic accounting softwares such as Quickbooks. It does take a number of weeks/months to figure things out, especially if you are using Sage for the very first time. It is best to have one of their experts guide you through things. The wide range of features is great, but it does open the door for some confusion for some people.
column10: With Sage being a cloud-based software, everything saves automatically. We had previously used Quickbooks desktop, and we had to backup the work we did each day. We are able to avoid this problem by using Sage. Sage is also great because we have our banks linked and the transactions automatically come in the banking cloud each day, where we can create journal entries from there.

column8:
Below are the points for your reference.
1. On the transactions related to the AP / AR Payment application it will not allow us to see the details for which invoice the payment has been received or Paid.
2. After AP / AR payment applications, if there are any Forex Gain / Loss arises the same issue it will not show the details on the screen about the invoice Number if it's updated it would be helpful.
Except above observations I have not found any reason to dislike Intacct.
column10:
1. Providing reports to clients has become easier with the reporting module for example Vendor / Customer agings, BS, and P&L reports.
2. Bank reconciliations, here we are using SIMPL integration with Intacct to do the reconciliations of almost 12 bank accounts it's helping us to update the transaction on a daily basis and perform the reconciliation.
3. Purchase Order module, our client is using the travel / Non-Travel PO module in Intacct from there our team is able to get the invoice to process in BILL, it reduces the communication chain happening to get the invoices. 
4. With all these updates we are able to provide the monthly reports to clients on time.
5. Doing consolidation with Multicurrency entities has become easier with one click button.

column7: The ability to load and invoice from Contracts - very strong.
column8: Invoice templates are set in place at the Customer level They need to be able to be set at the Contracts level. We have multiple Entities that share the same Customer (at the top level). However, we need the ability to generate unique invoices at the Contract level. Right now, we have to go into the Customer and select the invoice template several times a month to "flip-flop" so we can bill accordingly. A pain. Also, we just discovered that when using The Quanties in Usage templates, the system is rounding up. So an entry of "1.5" is being rounded up as a mulitplier of "2." When the unit price is $1000, that makes an enormous miscalculation.
column10: Coming from Quickbooks, the ability to have software that is Contract aware is game-changing.

Sage products are typically fantastic. Sage launched this product a little prematurely in my opinon. Each update to the software seems to be getting things to where they need to be.

Product is easy to navigate and there are multiple ways to get the same information from various reports. Transactional processing is also quite intuitive.
column8: Some canned reports from Sage 50 ($500, off the shelf program) are not in their expensive ERP. Sage missed the mark here. We are spending thousands with external consultants for what should have been included with the software... this is frustrating.
column10: The product has definitely afforded the company various efficiencies vs the legacy Sage 300 program that we were utilizing. The biggest time savings has come from parnering with Miter for our payroll and payroll tax processing.

column8: Setting up reports such that they generate specific drill-downs or selected statistics is not easy. This lack of flexibility – specifically, the inability to manipulate data precisely as I need it – can make my analysis inefficient or even necessarily require export to other software.
column10: Since Sage Intacct is a suite solution, it saves the need to have several accounting applications in the financial management process. Real-time reporting, improved thinking and scalability capabilities are key elements that make it central to its accounting functions.

column7: Sage Intacct allowed us to consolidate our accounting under one platform. Originally we were on seperate instnaces of quickbooks and this really improved our accounting. The utilization of intercompanies reduced the amount of time spent recodring transactions between entitites and ensured accurate balances. The reporting in Sage Intacct has been very robust. Not only is the reporting strong but it is also very user friendly to update and create new reports as needed. The ability to intergrate with outside platforms is also a huge plus for the system.
column8: There are a few nuances of things that can be challening. The credit card workflow while robust can feel burdensome and overly complicated. it is also challening that credit card transactions cannot be reclassed once paid which leads to the need for additional journal entires. Some items also cannot be changed once setup and this has lead to the need to recreate new bank accounts or credit cards because an incorrect location was selected.
column10: Sage Intacct allows us to have all of our accoutning and reporting under one system. It has helped us to streamline the accounitng process through better intergrations and more robust reporting. It is also relatively easy to use. Sage

column7: My favorite thing about Sage Intacct is how intuitive and easy to use it is. Everything from data entry to creating custom reports just makes sense. There is so much depth to Intacct that it's easy to make it a one stop shop ERP system rather than having to use multiple different sites/programs for different things. Having come from Netsuite and Xero previously, I've been able to pick up Sage Intacct very quickly and have become quick and efficient with it.
column8: The cost of some of the additional modules available are a bit steep but that seems to have become the industry norm so I'm not sure that is something to gripe about. Other than that the only thing I can think of is that the prepaid expense schedules are a bit more clunky than some other ones out there.
column10: Sage Intacct makes gathering, analyzing, and exporting data very easy. Almost any report that I'm asked to create can be done rather quickly and has made my job so much easier and more efficient. As an Accountant and Data Anaylist there are many different reports I need to be able to compile and with Intacct there is always a quick way to gather the data I need. Sage

column7: Sage Intacct has been a game-changer for our organization, and what I appreciate most about this cloud-based financial management software is its powerful and intuitive functionality that streamlines complex financial processes. One of the standout features is its advanced financial reporting capabilities. The ability to create customized, real-time financial reports is incredibly valuable. Sage Intacct’s built-in dashboards and reporting tools provide insightful analytics that help us make informed decisions quickly. The platform’s flexibility in generating tailored reports means we can address specific business needs without relying on extensive manual data manipulation. The automation features within Sage Intacct are incredibly beneficial. Automated workflows for routine tasks like invoice processing, expense management, and reconciliation reduce manual effort and minimize errors. This not only speeds up our financial operations but also allows our team to focus on strategic initiatives. The customer service is also welcomed and robust.
column8: The cost of Sage Intacct can be relatively high, especially for smaller organizations or those with tight budgets. Pricing is based on the number of users and features, which can add up.
column10: Sage Intacct provides robust financial management features that streamline our complex financial needs, including tracking multiple funding sources, managing restricted funds, and reporting on various grants and donations. This ensures accurate and transparent financial reporting, which is crucial for compliance with donor requirements and regulatory standards. It helps nonprofits maintain financial integrity and build trust with stakeholders.

column7: Sage Intacct stands out for several reasons, but one of the key features I appreciate is its robust cloud-based architecture, which provides real-time financial data and insights. This can be incredibly valuable for organizations that need up-to-date information to make informed decisions. Additionally, its flexibility in handling complex financial structures and its strong integration capabilities with other systems make it a powerful tool for managing finances efficiently. The automation of routine tasks and its user-friendly interface also help streamline processes and improve overall productivity.
column8: One aspect that might be a bit frustrating is the learning curve. While the system is powerful, getting to grips with all its functionalities can take time, which might be a bit of a hurdle if you're trying to get up to speed quickly. And as with any software, the experience can vary based on individual needs and how well the system aligns with those needs.

column10:
We use Sage Intacct to raise and approve purchase transactions, manage inventory transactions and to allocate payments and receipts. It is also used to provide key financial reports such as P&L, Balance Sheet, General Ledger, Trial Balance etc which allows us to reconcile our accounts.
Additionally, we make use of the Fixed Asset Tool within Intacct to post depreciation and maintain the fixed asset register.
Finally, we have built some ad-hoc custom reporting for business analysis purposes, such as committed spend, profitability analysis, etc

column7: I like the speed of navigation between the Applications, the ability to have drill down functionality to source documents while keeping the original application window open.
column8:
The training is orientated towards the US market and customers so trying to align it to the UK use is complex. Basic features (i.e. Customer refund button, VAT submission automatically updating the VAT accounts) which were in Sage 50 have been removed with complex or manual finance intervention required eroding time savings and accuracy of processing information as it relies on the individual understanding and working out how to process these adjustments/entries. 

Lots of modules look like they are from another product and just bolted on without the end user usability or finesse being added (i.e. PEA, Collections and Fixed Assets). The system is very configurable but admin heavy and a lot of system limitations meant we had to work out our own workaround solutions.
column10: Reporting visibility is better, more detailed granular level reporting capibility through the use of Dimensions, ability to have Parent child relationships. The Spend management module is particularly useful in trying to manage risk of overspending as requisitioners can see what they have spent/committed against the budget before raising a PO Sage

column7: What I like best about Sage Intacct is that it's modern and sleek with a very clean user interface that's easy to use. It also contains a lot of powerful features to make it easier to run our accounts department and perform various tasks such as raising invoices on the AR and AP side, making payment runs, sending out remits and statements, receiving payments and allocating them to invoices. Plus the system is cloud based which makes it easy to implement and with frequent updates and not having to install or update softare on localised machines makes it easy to integrate into the business.
column8: What I dislike about Sage Intacct is not being able to download same day transactions on the bank feed and instead we have to wait until the next day before we see them which can cause a delay in payment allocations. I've also noticed that we can't add in a PO number in the reference of an old invoice from a locked period, I can understand we can't amend the values because it can affect the financial reports but it's a bit frustrating not being able to edit the reference to have the PO added in which means I'll have to add it in manually using Adobe Acrobat Pro.
column10: As a Credit Controller, Sage Intacct makes it easy for me to run an aged debtors report at the click of a button and to zero in on unpaid overdue invoices based on 30 days, 30-60 or 90 day+ from invoice date. This allows me to focus on customers who are taking longer to settle their overdue invoices compared to other customers.

column7:
1. Customizations - The number of customizations we can make in the Sage Intacct is what I think makes it unique from other applications! totally love this feature, you guys make the customizations that we require in the FUTURE, but available to us NOW!
2. Navigation - The navigation is planned well and made easy even for those who are new to this application along with the warning messages even if something not to be done is about to be done! - resolve most of the errors we would likely make.
3. Download speed - It's one of the minute things I admire about Sage Intacct but still crucial one when things are in a hurry! Even with large amounts of data, the download speed is steady irrespective of it!
column8:
1. It would be more helpful if the upload error notification email consisted of more information on what went wrong in the data upload and the rectification steps to be taken in detail step-wise.
2. I would appreciate more expedited process in solving the issues reported to the customer support of Sage Intacct.

column7:
Our organization has full version of Sage Intacct but I am using AR part only.

it is best software to record large number of according transaction and very helpful to allocate the cash received.

we can easily track all pending AR items through AR aging.
column8:
customization sometime seems difficult.

if you wrongly applied payments to particular transactions then you have to manually reverse all the transactions one be one there is no other way which seems difficult sometimes.
column10:
We use integration with Salesforce all the invoices gets autosync to the software so we do no have to make much efforts to take the invoices into the software.


also the cash application is automated by our it team so we just have to check if all the transactions are perfectly recovered.

best part is that software is cloud bases so we can view and share the data accross the globe. Best

column7: Ease of use and automation capabilities. API documentation is also excellent and the SDKs are easy to use.
column8: The lack of having direct access to the database, which makes it a bit difficult to make changes on the back end for objects that are not available through the APIs.
column10: It eliminates the need for manual data capturing, as 3rd party systems can easily be integrated with Intacct.

column7: As a person that uses intacct literally every day for work purposes, I like the search engine. The search eginine is suprisingly easy to use and find what you are looking for if you have a rough detail or 2 regarding an entry. The differnt kind of reports you can make/ run is also a big asset. Especially when you need to find a lot of specifc items in a short amount of time. It also great to see all of our captiva invoices being imported into intacct in real time. the software seems almost limitless regarding tools you can integrate to make processing easier.
column8: I dislike the clunkiness of the attatchments tab. I wish there was a more seemless what of viewing an attchement for the entires i create. I also wish there was a way to download attachments directly from the vendor overview tab. THis would make vendor cleanup a lot easier in my opinion. A button to forceably drop reocurring payments immidiately would be amzing as well. Also a more "free" or "limitless" template of creating reports would defiently be a big asset for this platform.
column10: The biggest thing intacct helps me solve is organization. being able to upload attachments to intacct entires in a 1:1 ratio is amazing for record keeping. Our built in software, Captiva, that automatically imports completed bills into intacct is also a great asset and enforces this idea of 1:1 ratio for intacct entries. Review of

column7: It has great reporting. You can use several different dimensions and the different modules allow you to keep track of everything very easily. It is also highly customizable and can sync with many other systems.
column8: Customer refunds are difficult to create, and it takes a long time to completely learn how to use the software. I've been using it for 3 years and there is still so much I have to learn.
column10: It seems like they listen to their customers and try to make improvements.

Managing finances can be complicated, especially for growing businesses with multiple entities or locations. Intacct simplifies management by providing tools to track income, expenses, and other data across the company.
Without real-time visibility into data, businesses may struggle to make informed decisions. Intacct provides real-time access to information
Intacct helps businesses stay compliant by adhering to regulatory standards and providing detailed financial records. Its audit trail capabilities and built-in compliance features simplify the audit preparation process and ensure transparency.
Intacct helps us to resolve multiple accounting software issues other than international payments. The most useful thing about Intacct is help helps us to build financial data easily and the customization feature is the best among its competitors, reporting becomes easy for the adhoc requests we receive from clients that require lots of customisation Intacct is the best on this.

This system has streamlined our accounting processes so much. We are able to close our books much faster, and with having multiple entities all in the same system it has made our lives so much easier also.
This system is also helpful with the integrated products for online customer payments, the prepaids module, fixed asset module, etc. They are all easy to use and everything happens at the click of a button without having to log into multiple systems for data entry and reporting.
Customer support has been very helpful so far and the knowledge base center is a huge asset. In addition, I appreciate the timeliness of receiving responses from support.
column8: I don't have many things that I don't like about Sage Intacct other than wishing it was easier to reverse or reclassify information in certain transactions such as bills that have been paid, cash transactions, etc. Sometimes something just got coded to a wrong account and when I'm reviewing the data, it takes so much time to reverse an entry and redo it, rather than just being able to reclass a department, class, project, account, or amount.
column10: Already covered in answer about what I like about Sage. Those were all problems that Sage has solved for us, and the benefit has been providing us with faster closings and therefore faster month-end reporting. Sage

Using it since the start of the company and has worked great. Customer support is always there when we have a question and it serves our needs. The customizing of reports is so great. It is simple to use. It's easy to navigate around and the better part is it is easy to train on. The grant application process is much faster and more efficient thanks to Sage Intacct. There are many activities that we can now perform automatically that we previously had to do on paper, saving us not only time but also resources. 
Aegis Project Controls has increased the frequency of budget vs. actuals reporting 3x, from quarterly to monthly, thanks to ease of use in Sage Intacct Planning. We solve data problems using pragmatic approaches and leverage a proven ecosystem including Sage Intacct for ERP. Every Sage ERP Implementation begins with the planning process. With the SAP and Sage integration, you can get combined insights from both platforms for better segmentation.

column7:
I really enjoy the reporting part of Sage. We get a detailed look into the finance side and this gives us a good insights into the business accounts. I also like that it is cloud-bassed, so we can access it anywhere at all.

The automation and integration features are fab also. It has helped us cut down on manual work and keep everything running smoothly.

Overall, it's been a huge help in managing our finances well.
column8:
Getting Sage Intacct set up just right can be a bit tricky. It'd be helpful to have a simpler setup process I think. 

While the reporting is robust, it can be overwhelming for those new to the platform. Cost can be a hurdle for smaller businesses, so having more flexible pricing options would make it more accessible. And while the customer support team knows their stuff, sometimes they take a while to get back to you. It'd be great if they could speed things up a bit for a smoother experience.
column10: Sage Intacct solves the integration and consolidation needs of the business and it streamlines financial operations without manual work. It has real-time visibility and customizable reports which enable swift, data-driven decisions. The Automation tools reduces errors and boosts productivity, while scalability supports our growing needs. In short, Sage Intacct enhances accuracy, efficiency, and agility in financial management.

column7: It is a more modernised system with potential to be an all in one system for accountants. The reoccuring journals are a great feature which allows you to save time creating journals. The consolidation of accounts is a great way to review company accounts on a whole without any manipulation of data. To create new nominal codes can't be any easier and the account mapping allows us to easily implement our processes on recording transactions.
column8: The price tag of additional packages is something that maybe needed to be looked into
column10: To be easily look into the consolidated accounts with out outside manipulation. The ease of finding information i.e invoice numbers and the audit trails allows us to get to the bottom of each issue that may arise. Overview of

column7:
My organization picked up effeciency in our month end process. The Dynamic Allocation feature saved my accounting team nearly a whole day on our close cycle as we can now allocation building expenses by square footage with a click of the button. 

The Sage Intacct interface was a clean and easy implementation. This was largely impart due to the flexibility of the integrations using excel templates for almost every module. The system excels at importing and exporting data so we were able to quickly and efficently transfer from our legacy system with very little issue.
column8: The purchasing module is a little clunky, so we reverted back to the AP module to take advantage of the AP automations. Purchasing could be vastly improved if it had the same scanning features as the AP automation.
column10: The Dynamic GL ledger with the non-profit module really cleaned up our trial balance. In our legacy system we had to spin off so many accounts to track our grant spend. With the non-profit module I can spin up grants with start and stop periods without having to add GL lines, which has been a game changer for our financial reporting.

column7: We are using Intacct on a daily basis. The best part of Intacct is the user interface, which is very simple. We have all the modules, for example, the AP Module (PO Module, Expense Management Module, etc.). Any issues, we can reach out to customer support easily, the response is very quick, and the implementation was hassle-free. Also, we use a BILL for account-payable activities; from the BILL, we will integrate all the AP bills into Intacct. This process is also very simple, and we don't see any issues.
column8: The overall experience in Sage Intacct is positive. If we need to make a few improvements, I can say that the audit trail details can be improved by providing an in-depth audit trail. Once the payment is applied, we will not be able to make any changes to Bill. For certain levels of access (admin), they should have access to edit and correct; this will save a lot of time.
column10: In our organization, we have multiple entities with multiple currencies, and we do global consolidations.Intacct is supporting multiple entities and providing a great result in global consolidation. The financial automation has helped us save more time while also providing very accurate financials to our clients. This has helped us to improve the net promotor score, and clients are very happy with the results. Intacct is very cost-effective and has different packages based on business requirements. column7: Being able to mass print invoices or sales orders is a huge plus, and makes end of day work quicker than having to click into each invoice separately to print. Love that reports are easily categorized to pull information we need and export straight into excel.
column8: There is very little room, well no room for small error. To make a change on a task that is already posted, you cant edit, it must be deleted and redone. A/R is unato make any kind of adjustments before submitting a deposit such as a few cents being off and marking it as other income. We have to create a whole new invoice for a few cents. It would be wonderful if our items information was attached to other areas. If I am viewing an item, i should be able to see the last cost, retail, and what we sell it to our customers for...
column10:
Our system has 'gone down' a few times, customer service is fairly quick to get things resolved. Our business relys on the need to print sales orders a day ahead, pack orders, and invoice them all before leaving. We appreciae the timely responce in getting our issues fixed, well the system fixed.
One other thing I forgot to mention is that the maintenance they do on some Fridays kicks us out of the system at around 7:30pm, sometimes leaving us high and dry. We are in California so if they can make it for a little later we wouldnt have and issue.

column7: The flow of entries and Reporting of accounts is the best part of Intacct as we can easily integrate Intacct with multiple software and the entry gets posted to the desired account based on the mapping done in the backend. The best part is, that we can even pull the reports for a particular GL account, check the entries, and make changes wherever required.
column8: The payment application is one thing that needs to be improved as there are a lot of sync errors that I am experiencing. The few errors I got were the payment made in another platform was synced to Intacct but later when the same was voided, the reversal didn't get synced, and the partial payment made will also not get synced to Intacct. It's double work for us to reapply the payment in Intacct again. So I feel Intacct should work on the payment application process.
column10:
Intacct is the platform where I can store all the accounting data in one place. We can create multiple entities under one business account and later split the data accordingly. We can even see the consolidated financials at the top level. Intacct allows us to create the vendor at the top level and process the invoices at the entity level. We are using multiple platforms for processing vendor invoices and creating invoices for our customers, but all the data that is processed in other platforms will easily get synchronized to Intacct. Even more best part is, that when the employees submit their expense reports in the expense management platform, that data will also get synced to intacct once the manager approves the same. Overall I can say data is perfectly synced from different software to Intacct. 

Later, the data that is synced gets passed as entries in Ledger based on the mapping done in the backend. We can make any changes to the data unless the payment is applied because once the payment is applied, the only option is to reclassify the entries in the ledger and not in the bill copy.

Finally, most of my reporting is automated as Intacct provides us with the data based on what is required. So not worry about spending more time on reporting as everything is readily available.

column7:
We had out grown our previous accounting system which meant simple tasks like month end and processing of invoices had become cumbersome. Sage Intacct was a significant investment for us, and was selected after a comprehensive tender process.

We chose Sage Intacct because it allowed us to implement the system in a way that ran like our existing system, this allowed for easy adoption across the group. Once the business had accepted the change we have been able to steadily roll out extra functionality. Most recently the implementation of Fixed Assets and Prepayments module.

Sage Intacct has allowed us to transform the way people in the business see and interact with finance, and has meant that people are now engaged in a pro-active manner because they are able to self serve.

Implementation of Sage Intacct was a relatively smooth process, and we were supported by Sage specialists throughout. Ongoing support is good and responsive.
column8:
Sage Intacct is a complex system and it requires a bit of time to get your head around how the system works, once you (and your finance team) do get it, it is easy to add additional functionality because each module follows a similar logic.

Currently (for a UK based company) some of the system is still geared towards a US market, but that is changing, and Sage do listen and implement updates based on feedback.
column10:
Streamlining of processes to reduce month end timelines - through use of POs, Fixed Assets and Prepayments.

More visibility to our Project Managers, allowing them to really own in real time the P&Ls of the projects.

column10:
Sage Intacct Automation
Automation in financial processes has transformed the way we work. Tasks that take hours now just take minutes. This helps us handle clients with lots of transactions better, making them happier. It also boosts our reputation in the market and brings in more business. Automation makes our services better and make us more trusted. It's a win-win for both clients and the firm.
Sage Intacct Extensibility
In my current company, we use multi-entity and global consolidations, which have proven to be highly effective for accounting services. Clients are more satisfied when they have several entities, as it allows them to present their financials in a consolidated format or to investors. This greatly contributes to maintaining a positive and trustworthy reputation for the business.
The IT team in our company set up the Intacct for us. Now I use it every day to record financial transactions. When we get information from clients through mail or bills directly from vendors, I enter it in. The configuration was very easy when I checked with the internal team involved in the installation processes of Sage Intacct to users. One Stop

column7:
To begin with my review, I would firstly like to tell that this one amongst the most user friendly ERP software that i have come across. The interface, the navigation and quick access to required modules makes it more easier even for the newest users to the system and helps them to use the application very frequently without any hazard.

The features such as Projects/Contracts helps the business to track the progress and also to review their results in a much deeper way.

Also the integration of Intacct between various other applications such as BILL, Nexonia makes it more convenient for business users to perform their activities in their preferred application but still to review the financials in Intacct.

The option to track the audit trail and also to view the history of any page makes it safer for businesses as it indirectly warns any unauthorized user to make an inappropriate change.

column7: Intacct has streamlined the accounting process, offering a unified platform where I can easily access all necessary information. One of its standout features is the robust reporting functionality. Intacct not only handles the intricate design aspects of reports but also delivers clear, comprehensible reports that can be effortlessly shared with stakeholders.
column8: Intacct stands out as an exceptionally user-friendly software with impeccable design across its features. However, it's worth noting that there is one aspect that lacks user-friendliness: the inability to reverse a credit memo once it has been applied. This restriction remains unchanged in all instances.
column10: The data flow within Intacct is remarkably straightforward. I seamlessly integrate Bill.com for vendor invoice processing, ensuring effortless synchronization with Intacct. Similarly, Nexonia simplifies the submission of expense reports, which seamlessly sync with Intacct, resulting in successful entries. When it comes to checking the balance in any account, I can easily access specific balances under the General Ledger tab. Moreover, I rely on Intacct for creating various reports shared with stakeholders, consolidating all reporting tasks into one platform, and significantly reducing the time previously spent on using multiple software applications to prepare reports. column7: <v ->I am Braam.</v> I'm the controller. And it's for a nonprofit organization. And I give Sage Intacct, a huge mighty 10 out of 10. The problems that we are facing is one that also many that, so many people experiencing, is the fact that you want data in real time. What is happening now, as opposed to what has happened yesterday or month before. So that is data that you want to present in real time, and then also visibility. We want to make sure that the data is easily accessible, and where people can actually view it in their own comfort, in their own time, but then also at ease. Meaning that they can easily follow around by clicks and droning capability if they have any questions. So and that is the problem that we used to have with a legacy system that people just could not have data at real time, and then once they have it, or you're presented to them, or give them access to the system, they could not follow through because it was too hard to comprehend. The feature that I like best is that Intacct, or I should say, Sage Intacct is always forever evolving. Not so much that you cannot keep up, but it is changing with the times. Technology is moving so fast and so is processes that makes things a little bit easier. And what they do is they release enhancements quarterly, and in between they do sort of like software enhancements monthly, but major enhancements that you can actually review your processes, can be utilized and implemented. So that is something that makes it, software stays fresh, and it makes everyone to be engaged with and it also helps with collaboration with other stakeholders. The thing that I like the least about Sage Intacct is the feature where you have to attach documents. That is something that you know as accountants, we always have to do, you still need supporting documentation, even in this time where things are very much automated, and or in digital format, we still require to add some support documentation. Now the attachment feature, although very handy, I think it can somehow be great if documents can be automatically integrated without physically attached something. I don't have a specific example in how you can do it, but know that that would be great, if it is something that you can do easier, to attach supportive documentation. When considering using Intacct, I would strongly recommend to speak with other customers. Get a handful of customers, three to five, and really find out from them as to how they use the software. A great place also to start is to, if these annual meeting coming up soon, ask to be invited. If you are looking for a step up from Quickbooks for your business, Sage Intacct should be on your list of software options to look into. column8: The assets module needs an update, and the inventory kitting feature seems to have a lot of bugs, but all are still useful for the price of Sage Intacct. SI also needs more short keys to match the productivity of other systems like SAP. Performing a bank rec is all done with clicks and takes what seems to be an eternity.
column10:

column7: The product is wonderfully built. The support infrasturcture is terrible.
column8:
Where to begin? The customer service model is an absolute failure and has been for a year. It is very difficult to get a hold of anyone. Multiple phone calls go unreturned, or receive a response via email instead of return phone call, usually asking someone else on their team to reply to a question. It's a strange way to shirk responsibility, but it's constant. 

Their billing department sent unverified claims for fees that they could not support but continued to invoice every 30 days with no justification. It's borderline harassment and should be reported to the BBB.

We attempted to make payment for a balance they said was due, then they returned the payment and suspended our account 30 days later with no explanation or forewarning. Truly appaling way to treat a long-standing customer. 

To say that the company's support model is a failure would be an understatement. It has actively subverted our business objectives for some time, and I could not recommend a single company use them unless it is fixed. Which is a shame, because the product is really strong.
column10: Sage Intacct is actively creating problems through poor service delivery and an incomprehensibly bad A/R department. It is a profound irony that a company that built accounting software cannot produce any justification for a claimed A/R outstanding balance for months on end. Truly offensive.

column7: Sage Intacct's real-time access to financial data is one of its most noteworthy features. Because we can quickly obtain current financial information, this has been essential when making important decisions. The software offers a great platform for developing financial controls and guaranteeing adherence to accounting rules.Customer service from Sage Intacct has been prompt in responding and providing valuable answers. Our team has benefited greatly from their expertise and desire to help.
column8: While most of the complex capabilities of the software are easy to use, mastering them has taken some effort.For busy professionals who must access financial data on the go, expanding the features of the mobile app would be advantageous.It might be advantageous to offer more adaptable price structures or packages that are designed for various business sizes.
column10: Having real-time access to financial data has given us the ability to act quickly on data-driven decisions, improving financial results. Our finance operations have changed substantially as a result of Sage Intacct. Our financial processes' manual and time-consuming nature is one of the key issues it has resolved. column10:
Streamlining Accounting Processes: Sage Intacct addresses the challenge of manual and time-consuming accounting processes. By automating tasks such as data entry, transaction processing, and reconciliation, the software saves significant time and reduces the risk of human errors.

Enhancing Financial Visibility: Many businesses struggle with gaining real-time insights into their financial performance. Sage Intacct provides customizable dashboards and reports, allowing users to access up-to-date financial data, analyze trends, and make informed decisions promptly.

Scalability for Growth: As businesses expand, their accounting needs become more complex. Sage Intacct is designed to accommodate growth, providing scalability that enables companies to manage increased transaction volumes, multiple entities, and evolving financial requirements.

Ensuring Compliance and Security: Compliance with financial regulations is crucial for businesses of all sizes. Sage Intacct follows industry standards and employs robust security measures to protect financial data, mitigating the risks associated with non-compliance and data breaches.

Facilitating Collaboration: With cloud-based accessibility, Sage Intacct facilitates collaboration among team members, regardless of their location. This aspect is particularly beneficial for businesses with remote or distributed teams.

Integrating Business Applications: Sage Intacct's integration capabilities allow it to connect with other essential business applications like CRM, payroll, or inventory management systems. This seamless integration ensures that data flows smoothly across various platforms, minimizing data silos and improving overall operational efficiency.

Improving Financial Decision-making: By providing accurate and timely financial information, Sage Intacct empowers businesses to make well-informed decisions, strategize effectively, and identify areas for cost optimization and revenue growth.

Reducing IT Infrastructure Overheads: As a cloud-based solution, Sage Intacct eliminates the need for businesses to maintain and manage their own IT infrastructure. This results in cost savings, as cloud-based software typically requires fewer hardware investments and less maintenance. Sage Intacct - A Powerful Cloud-Based Accounting Software for

column7:
Intacct's General Ledger module is highly efficient for inputting and managing journal entries (the best I've personally used in my 20+ year accounting career).

Interface is clean and consistent across all modules (with the exception of the Collections module).

Database schema is easy to follow.
column8:
Invoice management is spread across multiple modules and needs to be made more lean. The Accounts Receivable and Order Entry modules both hold the same invoice and offer different capabilities, which sounds good in theory (more options is rarely bad), but unfortunately it creates too many opportunities for information to appear in one module but not the other. A favored reporting option may only be available under one module and not the other, leading to data integrity issues.

If you're thinking of using the Contracts module for managing your SaaS customers, one word - DON'T. It does not aggregate subscription information within the UI, forcing the reader to look elsewhere for information they should be able read at the source. You also have to jump over way too many hurdles to place restrictions on date ranges for any subscription changes. The module is simply not built for the modern subscription model; it harkens back to a time when perpetual licenses and maintenance agreements were common.
column10:
We have consistent information in the general journal, bank reconciliations are clean each month, and expenses are easy to locate and track. This has given us improved insight over our spending.

While the Contracts module does not ultimately work well for our company, we do appreciate the level of detail that is available. This has put us along the path to knowing exactly what we want from a billing system.

column10: Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payment, Bank Reconciliations, Trial Balances and General Ledgers. Invoices are received from vendors, client, lenders – this initiates the transaction. Radian enters the approved invoice into Intacct. This initiates the transaction, it is recorded in Intacct (system) AR Module. JEs are posted monthly and quarterly for items like PPD amortization, depreciation, accruals, etc. These entries are supported by schedules maintained by Radian. During the close process, these JEs are entered by the preparer and reviewed by the reviewer before they are posted to the ledger. My experience has been mostly good.

column7: Sage offers excellent UI, and for basic accounting and managing multiple companies' books and records, it's an excellent out-of-the-box experience. If during the sage demo, sage can walk you through EVERY process you currently do, and they actually show you the process instead of telling you it's possible or scheduling a call with a company that can customize sage for you will be happy with their product.
column8:
Ensure that all necessary tasks are completed before entering into a contract with Sage. It is crucial to have the Sage team address your specific system requirements before committing to their services or consider exploring alternative providers.

Customization in Sage is similar to NetSuite, so be prepared to pay a premium and negotiate a favorable contract upfront if their out-of-the-box solution does not meet your needs. It can be surprising that some Sage representatives may not possess a thorough understanding of their own system and may suggest solutions that are not entirely effective. If you engage their Professional Services team, be aware that it may take a week or two to begin working with them, and the quality of their work may range from unsatisfactory to average, at a $250/hr rate.

Unlike Microsoft, where you can find a variety of skilled developers at different price points that can deliver, locating affordable Sage developers can be challenging. The high hourly rates are not the primary concern, but rather the dissatisfaction of paying for partial completion of a task. Unfortunately, the customer success team at Sage may not be very helpful, as they might lack knowledge about their own platform. Therefore, do not rely solely on their customer success team or Technology services team to deliver.

Hindsight, I would have approached this entirely different or went with an entirely different platform had we known what we know now.

column7: I like Sage Intacct for its comprehensive reporting capabilities. Its intuitive design makes it easy to access key data within the programme quickly and efficiently, even without prior training or experience. Thanks to this intelligent platform management system, our department managers now have direct visual insight into relevant figures and are always up to date with any changes in activity.

column7: I like the flexibility of this platform as opposed to other, more rigid platforms I've worked with. In the past with other platforms, I couldn't just remove a bad entry. I would have to create reversals. Not having unnecessary reversals is helpful when it comes to the reconciliation processes. Because of this flexibility and the fact that there are rarely any reconciliation issues between the sub ledger and the general ledger, I'd highly recommend this software pack.
column8: What I dislike most about the software is the lack of customization of the platform. It took me three months to get a working invoice template from them. I would also like to add that the color schemes and company logo cannot show a semi-customizable platform. Creating a color coding system for divisions in the software would be most useful. They are getting along the way with some automated tasks, but there is still a long way to go to complete push-button automation.
column10: Managing multiple companies and bank accounts that do not have API's is most laborious. I would much rather have an AI discern items, and then the user reviews the AI's work for accuracy, including reading and reconciling a bank statement. Managing a workup for pre-paids processing by working out the expense period where possible without input from the user. Managing a detailed and easily reportable fixed assets schedule and entry detail, making disposition seemless. Essentially I would like the automation that would allow a Fortune 500 to be run by a single individual and an interconnected network of users that could invoice and pay across the platform.

column7: I particularly like the ability to drill down into real-time financial analysis and budgeting. Sage Intacct gives you unparalleled insight so you can make proactive decisions faster than ever before. You can view revenues and expenses by month or quarter; analyse variances between projected and actual performance with graphical representations for easy comparisons; create custom dashboards of key indicators such as accounts receivable versus sales orders, different types of activity related numbers that are continuously monitored along with many other available metrics within the system all provide amazing capabilities that bring a business closer to being 'fully informed' at every decision point.
column8: I find it frustrating that the project and task columns in Sage Intacct are so limited. With no means of expanding them, all names are truncated at a certain length and become difficult to differentiate when they start with similar titles. The same problem exists with the Tasks column, making it difficult for me to interpret the data efficiently.
column10: I use Sage Intacct to facilitate financial analysis, budgeting, and reporting. I've found the reconciliation module to be particularly helpful; it has enabled me to streamline my financial operations in a shorter period of time. The best accounting

column7: Sage's strength is its ease of use. Modules and applications are laid out logically, and the ability to customize workflows or create new workflows and reports based on company needs is fantastic. Couple that with the best online support documentation that I have seen, and you could competently build or rebuild an instance of Sage with just the written online documentation
column8: What can be frustrating about Sage is the complexity of custom reports. While the options for custom report creation are almost endless, the dizzying amount of options also makes custom reports tedious and challenging to generate. Creating an additional layer in custom reports with streamlined options for quick report creation while leaving the detailed option that currently exists would be an improvement to Sage.
column10: For us, Sage is a flexible cloud-based solution. Sage provides many options for how data can be labeled and stored. These options give the user multiple ways to pull reports and data from different angles to help make the best analysis possible. Our company is spread out and operates many small locations so the cloud-based system allows access to the system anywhere there is interet.

column7: I've been using Sage Intacct for six years now to handle accounts payable for our church. In addition to making expenses tracking a breeze for us, I appreciate the "duplicate last check" feature as many of our bills are the same each month. The Reports feature is SOOOO helpful at tax time to help us find vendors that are eligible for W9's. It also has a multiple campus feature which will come in handy when we launch a second church campus in the near future. Would definitely recommend giving this software a look for accounts payable needs!
column8: It would be great if we could have the ability to adjust where the memo line appears on our checks. We recently changed check vendors and the memo now prints in the border of the check. Unfortunately after speaking with Intacct I found that this feature was not available.
column10: Tracking our expenses by vendor and fund each year. The robust reports feature makes it easy to customize the data so you get all of what you need and none of what you don't.

column8: The back-end mapping needed can get convoluted and like all software, you don't know what you don't know until you run into an issue. One of the areas that I believe need to be built out better is the audit trail. It doesn't seem to tag people's edits on certain fields, so tracking who did what change can be difficult. Another area isthe template upload error emails. If there is an error in the CSV upload, the email notification on the reason why can be unhelpful in knowing what to correct in the next try.

column7: I especially appreciate the custom reporting features in Sage Intacct. The intuitive interface makes it easy for non-financial staff to navigate and use. Since we are determined to stay with Sage Intacct for many years to come, having options such as budgeting tools will be extremely helpful in the future - not forgetting smart rules/events that can provide additional functionality in the future.
column8: I noticed that the transition process included several redundant or duplicate steps that were not helpful. column7: I like using Dimensions; they are mighty. As a nonprofit, we migrated from Quickbooks to Intacct in 2021 for two reasons: 1. We can track grant restrictions 2. Dynamic allocations.
column8:
1. Export to excel does not include formulas (I waste so many hours recreating formulas it's infuriating)
2. Implementation required a third party that messed up our configuration, and there was no help to fix the configuration error, which cost additional money for another third party to correct. 
3. Quickbooks credit card processing was streamlined; Intacct does not have a native cc processor, and one has to build one for Stripe with a third party.
4. Product releases are confusing and slow. Announcement of Artificial Intelligence for Billing in 2021, but did not follow through with release until 2023, with no straightforward implementation instructions.
5. Dashboards are not very dynamic and visually unappealing.
6. Basic dashboard are weak compared to QuickBooks.
7. Report writing is complex and not user-friendly. Quickbooks out of the box reports are super easy in comparison.

column7:
Intacct provided a valuable solution to nonprofit accounting compared to the older financial systems that were being used. The cloud based system allowed my teams to work effectively at home without worry of interruption regardless of where you are. We were fortunate enough to have already implemented Intacct before the pandemic. Even now that the pandemic has became an afterthought, Intacct still contributes greatly to our hybrid work model.

Intacct's ability to incorporate other softwares for direct feed is also an amazing function. I have linked Intacct to various tools such as Expensify, Adaptive Insights, Sage Intacct Planning (Budget), Salesforce, Ariett, etc.
column8: Intacct has a great foundation, but to tailor to your company's needs, you may need a lot of additional tools which could add up in costs.

column7: Sage has a strong financial focus and would work very well in a consulting services or Time and Materials situation. It struggles with project management and project finance and has limited functionality in that area.
column8:
The report writing and dashboard functionality is very outdated and made worse by inflexible data structures. Certain data tables can only be brought into certain report types, and there are line-item maximums to the number of dimensions items which can be added to groups/heirarchies. 
The release program for Sage updates is also problematic. Even Sage's premier partners struggle to facilitate client release preview environment testing, and the preview environments can't be integrated with integrations, which limits the ability to test. Our company has experienced two critical integration errors over the last three updates due to the lack of this ability to test.

column7: I love the convenience and flexibility of Sage Intacct's features. The ability to quickly retrieve historical data, reference materials, draft entries, and upload files for longer entries makes it easier than ever to stay on top of accounting tasks. I also appreciate its intuitive nature, which requires minimal training before I can easily master running a GL activity or making manual filing changes. Two of my favorite features are duplicating previous entries and automatically modifying memo fields by changing dates that I may have missed manually.
column8: I find the truncated project and task names in Sage Intacct extremely frustrating. It is difficult to distinguish between similar projects or tasks when they are only partially visible, making it difficult to quickly select the one I need. The inability to expand these columns exacerbates this problem - it would be much more helpful if there was a way to see the entire name, rather than being limited by its length.

This application has got the ability to control, adjust, and customize everything. It gives us the ability to be able to set everything up the way we want it to be. It's a robust and outstanding product where everything about the system is custom tailored and we've actually created read only access for our committee in Finance Department to improve our reporting as well as access for auditors.

column7:
Automation: Sage Intacct automates many routine accounting processes, such as accounts payable and accounts receivable, reducing the need for manual data entry and increasing accuracy.

Integration: Sage Intacct integrates with a range of other software, including CRM, ERP, and payroll systems. This allows for seamless data transfer and reduces the need for manual data entry.

Customization: Sage Intacct is highly customizable, allowing users to configure the system to meet their specific needs. This includes custom reports, workflows, and dashboards.
column8: The challenge is that the cost of using Sage Intacct may be prohibitive for some organizations, particularly small businesses or nonprofits with limited budgets. Organizations should carefully consider the costs of implementing and maintaining the system before deciding to use it.
column10:
Manual processes: traditional accounting involves a lot of manual processes, including data entry and reconciliations. Sage Intacct automates many of these processes, reducing the risk of errors, improving accuracy, and freeing up staff time to focus on more strategic tasks.
Lack of visibility: organizations may struggle to keep track of their financial performance in real-time, particularly if they have multiple locations or departments. Sage Intacct provides real-time visibility into financial data, including cash flow, revenue, and expenses. A very good experience with
